recalls.Description: AirClub has issued a recall for its convertible bassinets, which can also function as bedside sleepers. Available in light grey, beige, and purple, these bassinets feature quilted padding and are marked with the product model QX-831 on a label located beneath the mattress pad. The design poses a significant risk as it does not meet mandatory safety standards, creating a dangerous gap between the sleeper and adult mattress that can lead to falls.
recalls.Title: AirClub Convertible Bassinets Recalled for Fall Hazard Risk Leading to Serious Injury or Death
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach AirClub at 833-977-6667 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email support@airclub-baby.com, or visit https://airclub-baby.com/pages/recall or https://airclub-baby.com/ and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled AirClub Convertible Bassinet – Front View light grey
Recalled AirClub Convertible Bassinet - Rear View
"Product Model: QX-831" is printed on a label located on bottom of the convertible bassinets underneath the mattress pad
"Product Model: QX-831” is printed on a label located on bottom of the convertible bassinets underneath the mattress pad
Recalled AirClub Convertible Bassinets L-Shaped Straps Attachment Mechanism

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from October 2023 through December 2025 for between $110 and $130.

Distributors.Name: Zhangzhou Qiangxing Supply Chain Management Co., Ltd. dba AirClub of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to gap between bedside sleeper and adult mattress

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the bassinets and contact AirClub for a replacement mechanism, which involves removing the original straps and installing new ones.

recalls.Description:
Goregent has issued a recall for their infant walkers due to a significant fall hazard that could result in serious injury or even death. These walkers, featuring a green design with an animal print fabric seat, include a rotating activity tray equipped with toys, lights, and music. They are collapsible and have three adjustable height settings, making them versatile for different stages of infant growth. The model number '901' and SKU 'GEBA030AGXP' can be found on a yellow label on the base of the walker, with a production date of November 2025.

These infant walkers do not comply with mandatory safety standards, as they can pass through standard doorways and do not stop at the edge of steps. This poses a dangerous risk of falls. Parents and caregivers are urged to cease use of the product immediately to ensure the safety of their children.
recalls.Title: Goregent Infant Walkers Recalled Due to Fall Hazard Risk Leading to Serious Injury or Death
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Goregent Official Store via email at GoregentInfantWalkersRecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Goregent Infant Walker

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com in January 2026 for about $90.

Distributors.Name: Dongguanshi Aokaolan Trading Co., Ltd., dba Goregent Official Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from fall hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled walkers and contact Goregent Official Store for a full refund. Disassemble the walker, remove the seat, mark 'Recalled' on the tray, and send a photo to GoregentInfantWalkersRecall@outlook.com.

seo.metaDescription: Goregent recalls infant walkers due to serious fall hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: HALO is recalling its Magic Sleepsuits due to a potential choking hazard. The affected sleepsuits, identified by batch codes PO30592, PO30641, and PO30685, feature a shell and lining made from 100% cotton, with a fill of 100% polyester. These garments include double zippers on each side and are available in various colors and sizes. The name 'HALO Magic Sleepsuit' is displayed on the front, with batch codes and 'Made in India' noted on the internal label and hang tag.
recalls.Title: HALO Dream Recalls Magic Sleepsuits Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact HALO Dream at 833-791-0420 between 9 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, via email at customerservice@sleepsuitrecall.com, or visit www.sleepsuitrecall.com. More details can also be found at www.halosleep.com under the 'Recalls' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled HALO Magic Sleepsuit
The sewn in label with the batch code and "Made in India" circled
The hang tag with the batch code and "Made in India" circled

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the zipper head detaching from the garment. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Halosleep.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com and Target.com from September 2025 through February 2026 for about $50.

Importers.Name: HALO Dream, Inc., of New York City

Hazards.Name: Zipper head detachment posing a choking risk to infants.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled sleepsuit immediately and visit http://www.sleepsuitrecall.com to register for the recall. After registration, you will receive a coupon code for a replacement sleepsuit or a $50 credit for other items. Please do not discard the recalled sleepsuit until you have received your coupon code.

recalls.Description: Trankerloop is recalling its baby bath seats available in blue, gray, pink, and yellow due to serious safety concerns. These seats, which feature two detachable arms for restraint and four suction cups for stability, have been found to be unstable and can tip over during use, increasing the risk of drowning. Each seat is labeled with "PLASTIC STOOL" and "Model: YD-1958" on a tracking label located on the back.
recalls.Title: Trankerloop Baby Bath Seats Recalled Due to Drowning Hazard and Non-Compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Trankerloop at 405-204-8540 from 9 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT Monday through Friday or via email at hkkll147@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Trankerloop Baby Bath Seat – gray
"PLASTIC STOOL" and "Model: YD-1958" on tracking label on the back of the seat

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshirongmanshangmaoyouxiangongsi (Shenzhen Shi Ronmang Trading Co., Ltd.) dba Trankerloop, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to drowning from unstable bath seats.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bath seats immediately and contact Trankerloop for a full refund. Mark the front of the seat as 'Recalled', disassemble it, cut the suction cups, and email a photo of the disassembled seat to hkkll147@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: The Babysense Max View Baby Monitor, model number VBM55, is being recalled due to a potential fire hazard associated with its display unit. This unit, which allows parents to monitor their baby, features "5.5" HD 1080P" on the upper left of the screen and the "babysense" logo on the lower part. The model number, "VBM55RX," can be found on the identification label on the back of the unit. It is crucial that consumers do not dispose of this recalled device in regular trash or recycling bins, as the lithium-ion batteries require special handling to mitigate fire risks. Check with local hazardous waste centers for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Recall of Babysense Max View Baby Monitor Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Hisense at (833) 689-2652 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at support@recallsecure.com, or visit www.recallsecure.com or www.babysensemonitors.com and select "Product Recalls" for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Babysense Max View 5.5" Baby Monitor display/parent unit
Recalled Babysense Max View 5.5" Baby Monitor display/parent unit - back view
Recalled Babysense Max View Baby Monitor with included cameras

Injuries.Name: Hisense has received 11 reports of incidents involving the display unit for the Babysense Max View Baby Monitors. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Hisense Ltd., of Israel

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and babylist.com from January 2023 through December 2025 for between $90 and $180.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating or sparking of the display unit during charging

Remedies.Name: Stop using the display unit and request a free replacement from Hisense

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace is recalling its sensory swing designed for children due to a serious risk of strangulation. This swing features a soft, cocoon-style hammock that is intended for indoor use, available in various colors including green, gray, blue, and pink, as well as themed prints like ocean, jungle, space, and fairytale. Each swing comes with installation hardware and is packaged in a blue drawstring storage bag that displays the TheKiddoSpace logo.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Children's Sensory Swing Over Strangulation Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit their website at thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/sensory-swings. You can also find more information by clicking on 'Product Safety Recalls' at the bottom of their homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace Sensory Swing - green
Recalled TheKiddoSpace Sensory Swing in use - jungle print

Retailers.Name: Online at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com from November 2023 through February 2025 for between $50 and $80.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Risk of strangulation due to fabric loops that can entangle a child's head or neck.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the swing, remove it from children's access, and contact TheKiddoSpace for a full refund. Consumers must cut the swing's fabric in half, send a photo of the destroyed swing to info@thekiddospace.com, and properly dispose of it.

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace has issued a recall for its children's Christmas tree toy play sets due to potential lead exposure. These felt play sets feature a two-sided Christmas tree that stands approximately 40 inches tall and 28 inches wide, along with holiday-themed ornaments, including one that says "Merry Christmas," and a storage box. The product packaging displays the TheKiddoSpace logo and the phrase "Christmas Tree."
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Children's Christmas Tree Toy Play Sets Due to Lead Exposure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/christmas-tree. Alternatively, you can go to www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Children's Christmas Tree Toy Play Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace Children's Christmas Tree Toy Play Set

Retailers.Name: Online at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com from November 2023 through February 2025 for between $13 and $40.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: The zipper pull on the music box compartment contains lead levels that exceed federal safety standards, posing a risk of toxicity if ingested by young children. Additionally, the Christmas tree does not meet essential mechanical and electrical safety requirements for battery-operated toys.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled play sets, remove them from children, and contact TheKiddoSpace for a complimentary replacement toy of equal or greater value, including free shipping. To facilitate the process, consumers must mark the tree with the word "RECALLED" and send a photo of the marked item to info@thekiddospace.com. Afterward, the tree and storage box should be disposed of according to local waste regulations.

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace has issued a recall for their peg doll toys due to a serious choking hazard. These toys, designed for children, include seven colorful wooden peg figures, a matching cup, and a circular wooden tray. The toys are packaged in a blue box featuring the TheKiddoSpace logo and the title "Peg Dolls in Cups."

recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit their website at www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/peg-dolls. recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace has issued a recall for their Montessori egg puzzle toys designed for children. These toys, which include six or twelve white plastic eggs that can be split in half and are stored in a yellow case, pose a serious choking hazard for children under three years old. Each egg half features a unique geometric shape that matches only with its corresponding piece, but the size of the eggs can block a child's airway, leading to potential choking and even death.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Montessori Egg Puzzle Toys Due to Choking Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled toys immediately and remove them from children's reach. For further assistance, contact TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com, or visit their website at www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/geometry-egg. More information can also be found by clicking on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of their homepage.

Remedies.Name: Free replacement toy or USB cable; disposal instructions provided

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace has issued a recall for its children's flashcard talking toys due to safety concerns involving lead and phthalate exposure. The recall affects two types of toys: animal-shaped pastel toys and rectangular toys, both of which come with a set of colorful flashcards featuring various illustrations. The toys are designed to allow children to insert flashcards and hear corresponding sounds, but they have been found to exceed federal safety standards for lead and phthalates, posing potential health risks to young children.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Children's Flashcard Talking Toys Due to Lead and Phthalate Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled toys and contact TheKiddoSpace at info@thekiddospace.com or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/flashcards for further assistance.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Lead and phthalate exposure risks exceeding federal safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Free replacement toy or USB cable upon return of the recalled product.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to KiddoSpace-branded fingerpainting kits, which include a round paint palette available in sets of 12, 25, or 36 colors, along with one or more coloring books featuring 14 to 40 pages. The product packaging is light blue and displays the TheKiddoSpace logo, the title 'Finger Painting Kit,' an image of a child with painted hands, and the number of colors included. These kits pose a risk of injury due to the presence of harmful substances.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Children's Fingerpainting Kits Due to Hazardous Substances and Phthalate Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com, or visit their website at www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/finger-painting-kit or www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on 'Product Safety Recalls' at the bottom for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace Finger Painting Kit - 25-color Paint Palette
Recalled TheKiddoSpace Finger Painting Kit - 12-color Paint Palette
Recalled TheKiddoSpace Finger Painting Kit - 36-color Paint Palette

Injuries.Name: TheKiddoSpace has received 18 reports worldwide, including one in the United States, of strong paint odor, skin irritation and swollen lymph nodes.

Retailers.Name: Online at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com and Amazon.com from June 2023 through February 2025 for between $20 and $30.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: The fingerpainting kits contain hazardous substances such as methanol and ethylene glycol, violating the Federal Hazardous Substances Act. They also include a regulated phthalate, which breaches the federal phthalate ban and poses toxicity risks if ingested by young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately cease using the recalled fingerpainting kits, ensure that children do not have access to the products, and contact TheKiddoSpace for free replacement kits, including shipping. Consumers are required to dispose of the original kits following local regulations and send a photo of the disposed items to info@thekiddospace.com.

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace is recalling its handwriting practice kits, which include both block and cursive styles, due to hazardous lead levels in the pens. The block kits feature four reusable books adorned with animal illustrations, while the cursive kits contain three reusable books focusing on letters, words, and sentences. Both types come with various colored pen grips and refills, all marked with TheKiddoSpace branding. The presence of lead in the pen nibs poses a significant health risk to children, as lead is toxic when ingested.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Handwriting Practice Kits Due to Lead Exposure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately discontinue use of the affected handwriting kits and remove the pens and refills from children. For free replacements, contact TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/handwriting-kit. Additional information can be found by clicking "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the website.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Lead exposure from pen nibs exceeding federal safety limits poses health risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive free replacement pens and refills after disposing of the recalled items as per local regulations.

recalls.Description: Bicystar has issued a recall for their children's high chairs due to serious safety concerns. These gray high chairs feature a metal frame, metal legs, a tray, and a cushioned seat cover. The brand name "BICYSTAR" is displayed on the front of the tray, while the model number "TB-HC6900" can be found on a label stitched to the back of the seat cover.
recalls.Title: Bicystar High Chairs Recalled Due to Fall and Entrapment Risks; Noncompliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bicystar Store via email at bicystarhighchair@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bicystar High Chair - model TB-HC6900
"BICYSTAR" and "Model No.: TB-HC6900" are printed on labels stitched to the back of the recalled Bicystar high chair seat cover

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 1, 2025 through September 30, 2025 for about $76.

Distributors.Name: Hebei Beihong Kejiyouxiangongsi, dba Bicystar Group Co., Ltd. and Bicystar Store, of China

Hazards.Name: These high chairs lack the necessary attached crotch restraint, creating a significant fall hazard for children. Additionally, there is a risk of a child's head becoming trapped between the seat and the chair's side, leading to a dangerous entrapment situation.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled high chairs immediately and reach out to Bicystar Store for a full refund. They should disassemble the chair, cut the restraints and fabric seat cover, label the backseat with "Recall," and email photos of the destroyed high chairs to bicystarhighchair@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: Sanven Technology has announced a recall of Vevor-branded baby gates due to a serious entrapment hazard. These safety gates, available in gray, black, and white, feature a metal frame measuring approximately 34.2 inches in height. The retractable screens come in various widths: model YJ-3355 extends up to 60 inches, model YJ-3371 up to 76.8 inches, and model YJ-33110 up to 116 inches. The brand name 'Vevor' is displayed on the gate's corner post, while the model number can be found on the packaging and in the user manual.
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for Vevor Baby Gates Due to Entrapment Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, or via email at recalling@vevor.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.vevor.com/pages/Vevor-Retractable-Baby-Gates-Recall or by visiting www.vevor.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, Vevor.com, Walmart.com and Wayfair.com from December 2023 through December 2025 for between $32 and $53.

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., dba Vevor, of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to a child's torso fitting through the gap between the gate and the floor.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the gates immediately and contact Sanven Technology for a full refund. Additionally, they should destroy the gate by cutting through the mesh and email a photo of the destroyed gate to recalling@vevor.com.

recalls.Description:
Meijer has announced a recall of various styles of one-piece footed sleepwear for children in sizes 12, 18, and 24 months. These garments, branded under Lullaby Lane and MCS, have been found to violate safety standards regarding flammability, which could pose a burn risk to young children. The specific styles include a range of colors and patterns, with identifying details printed on the neck and side-seam labels.

Consumers are advised to check their sleepwear for the brand and style numbers to determine if they are affected by this recall. The safety of children is a top priority, and immediate action is recommended to prevent any potential hazards associated with these products.
recalls.Title: Meijer Issues Recall for Lullaby Lane and MCS Children's Sleepwear Due to Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Meijer at 800-927-8699 or visit https://www.meijer.com/recalls/KB0020513. Additional details can be found by clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the Meijer homepage.

Hazards.Name: Violation of mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear, leading to burn injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Return the recalled sleepwear to any Meijer store for a full refund.

recalls.Description:
CheerKid baby bath seats are being recalled due to a serious safety hazard. These bath seats, available in gray, pink, and light blue, feature five suction cups for stability and a colorful rolling abacus toy. The model number BH-222 can be found on a label on the back of the seat. Unfortunately, these products do not meet mandatory safety standards, making them unstable and prone to tipping, which increases the risk of drowning for young children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these bath seats immediately and reach out to Babibaby for a full refund. To ensure safety, mark the front of the seat with 'Recalled' using a permanent marker, disassemble the seat, and dispose of the components as instructed. This action is crucial to prevent any potential accidents.
recalls.Title: Recall of CheerKid Baby Bath Seats Due to Drowning Risk; Sold by Babibaby and Woot
recalls.ConsumerContact: Contact Babibaby via email at CheerKid-Recall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled CheerKid Baby Bath Seat – front view
Recalled CheerKid Baby Bath Seat - sideview
Recalled CheerKid Baby Bath Seat – bottom view
"Model: BH-222" is printed on a label on the back of the seat

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com in September 2025 for about $35.

Distributors.Name: Hangzhouyoupengmaoyiyouxiangongsi (Hangzhou Youpeng Trading Co., Ltd.), dba Babibaby, of China
Woot, of Carrollton, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to drowning from unstable bath seats.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bath seats, contact Babibaby for a refund, and follow disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: Hobby Lobby is recalling its Giraffe Plush Toy and Llama Plush Toy, both featuring clips. The Giraffe has a yellow body and measures approximately nine inches long, with a yellow claw clip and colorful legs. It includes identifiers HL699436 or HL731481 on the sewn-in label and 2553675 on the hang tag. The Llama, measuring about twelve inches long, has a white body with a green claw clip, a mirror, and an orange triangle teether. Its sewn-in label displays HL699436 and the hang tag shows 2553634. These toys pose a choking risk as the legs can detach, releasing small beads.
recalls.Title: Hobby Lobby Recalls Giraffe and Llama Plush Toys Due to Choking Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Hobby Lobby Stores at 800-326-7931 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email customer.service@hobbylobby.com, or visit www.hobbylobby.com/customer-service/recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled Giraffe Plush Toy w/Clip
Recalled Llama Plush Toy w/Clip

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the leg detaching from the body of the plush toy, allowing the small beads to detach from the leg. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Hobby Lobby Stores nationwide and online at hobbylobby.com from May 2025 through December 2025 for between $6 (Giraffe) and $8 (Llama).

Importers.Name: Hobby Lobby Stores, Inc. of Oklahoma City, Oklahoma

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to detachable legs releasing small beads

Remedies.Name: Return for a full refund

recalls.Description: The AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toy has been recalled due to a choking hazard that poses a risk of serious injury or death. This toy features an off-white disc shape with a red or blue ball in the center and six colorful pull string tentacles. It also includes three spinning rings and soft push buttons on the disc, with the model number 688-59 printed on the packaging.
recalls.Title: AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toys Recalled Due to Choking Hazard; Sold by Vanfun on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Vanfun via email at recallmls@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Teether Toy – Red
Recalled Teether Toy – Blue

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of 15 choking incidents and two incidents of children able to bite pieces off the silicone strings.

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2022 to September 2025 for about $10.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Mailesi Technology Co., Ltd., dba Vanfun, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to non-compliance with toy safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy immediately and request a full refund from Vanfun. Cut and discard all string tentacles, mark the toy as "DESTROYED," and email a photo of the destroyed item to recallmls@outlook.com.

These gyms can be converted from an activity center to a tummy-time pillow mat and were sold in pink, featuring a toy bar, six removable plush toys, and milestone cards. The product is labeled with "Baby Gym" and "Item No.: CBPBG-PIN" on a stitched label at the mat's edge.

recalls.Description: Aborder Products has issued a recall for Cumbor-branded retractable safety gates due to a significant risk of entrapment that could lead to severe injury or death in children. These gates feature a metal frame with a mesh screen, standing approximately 34 inches tall and adjustable up to 55 inches wide. Available in black, gray, and white, they include one or more suction cups for installation. The model name 'Retractable Safety Gate' and model number 'S5V2' can be found on a label attached to the mesh screen.
recalls.Title: Cumbor Retractable Safety Gates Recalled by Aborder Products Due to Serious Entrapment Risk for Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Aborder Products at 800-539-9594 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@cumbor.cn, or visit https://cumborgates.com/recall/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Cumbor retractable safety gate - gray
Suction cup sold with gate
Label affixed to the retractable safety gate fabric

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com in September 2025 for about $40.

Importers.Name: Aborder Products of Irving, Texas

Hazards.Name: Entrapment risk due to gap between gate and floor, violating safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates immediately, contact Aborder Products for a full refund, and destroy the gate by cutting the mesh, then email a photo of the destroyed gate to recall@cumbor.cn.

recalls.Description:
The Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy has been recalled due to serious safety concerns. This toy features an off-white disc shape with a red or blue ball at its center, accompanied by six colorful silicone pull string tentacles. Additionally, it includes three spinning rings and soft push buttons. The product packaging indicates the model number 688-59.

The recall is prompted by violations of mandatory toy safety standards, as the silicone strings are smaller than allowed, creating a choking risk that could lead to respiratory distress or even death. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toys Recalled for Choking Hazard; Sold by Longyanguiheng on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Longyanguiheng via email at yetonamr_recall@163.com.

recalls.Description: Greatale has issued a recall for their self-feeding pillows due to serious safety concerns. These pillows, designed to hold a bottle in front of an infant, can pose a risk of aspiration and suffocation as they keep the bottle at an unsafe angle, preventing the child from pulling away. The pillows are white and feature angel wing decorations, with the brand name "Greatale" labeled on the seam.
recalls.Title: Greatale Recalls Self-Feeding Pillows Due to Aspiration and Suffocation Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Greatale via email at greatale-recall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Greatale Self-Feeding Pillow

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 2024 through July 2025 for between $10 and $20.

Importers.Name: Xiamen Weiqiaoerwangluokeji Co., Ltd., dba Greatale, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of aspiration and suffocation due to unsafe bottle positioning

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pillows immediately and contact Greatale for a full refund. Consumers must cut the pillow and send a photo of the destroyed item, including its tag, to greatale-recall@outlook.com. If the tag is missing, proof of purchase from Amazon can be provided.

recalls.Description: Yixing Trading has announced a recall of KEAWIS crib mattresses due to serious safety concerns. These mattresses, sold in white and measuring approximately 38 inches by 24 inches, may not fit securely in certain play yards or non-full-sized cribs. This improper fit can create hazardous gaps that pose risks of entrapment and suffocation for infants. The model number "XX-BM-01" can be found on a label sewn onto the side of the mattress.
recalls.Title: Yixing Trading Issues Recall for KEAWIS Crib Mattresses Due to Entrapment and Suffocation Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled mattresses and reach out to Yixing Trading for a complete refund. Contact them via email at support@keawis.com.

recalls.Description: BBWOO baby loungers have been recalled due to serious safety concerns that could lead to injury or death. These loungers feature a foam sleeping pad, padded bumpers, and a cloth cover, with a ribbon tie at one end. The model number can be found on a tracking label stitched to the outer cover. The recalled styles include Gray Dinosaur, Blue Car, Green Line, Gray Feather, Pink Deer, Pink Elephant, Gray Line, and several others.
recalls.Title: BBWOO Baby Loungers Recalled Due to Serious Injury or Death Risks; Non-Compliant with Infant Sleep Product Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should email LSY Direct at bbwoorecall@163.co.

Hazards.Name: The loungers fail to meet safety standards for Infant Sleep Products, with low sides that cannot contain an infant and oversized openings that create fall and entrapment risks. Additionally, the lack of a stand presents a fall hazard when used on elevated surfaces, making these products unsafe for infant sleep.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the loungers immediately and reach out to LSY Direct for a full refund. They should also remove the foam and pads from the cover, then cut the cover, foam, and pad in half. To process the refund, consumers need to email photos of the destroyed items to bbwoorecall@163.com.

The model number ZY2025 is labeled on the side of the seat.

recalls.Description: Kalencom Corporation is recalling its SARO Braided Crib Bumpers, Model 1374, available in salmon, gray, and mint colors. These crib bumpers have "Ref. 1374" printed on a sewn tag and on the product packaging. The recall is prompted by safety concerns as these bumpers violate the federal ban on crib bumpers due to the risk of obstructing an infant's breathing, which can lead to serious injury or death from suffocation. This situation creates an unsafe sleeping environment for infants, violating the Safe Sleep for Babies Act.
recalls.Title: Kalencom Corporation Issues Recall for SARO Braided Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Kalencom Corporation at 800-344-6699 from 9 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kalencombaby.com/recall. Alternatively, they can visit www.kalencombaby.com and click on the recall tab.

Images.Caption: Recalled SARO Braided Crib Bumpers in gray, mint and salmon
Location of product tags showing "Ref. 1374"

Retailers.Name: MacroBaby, of Orlando, Florida and online at Kalencombaby.com and Target.com from May 2024 through October 2025 for about $60.

Importers.Name: The Kalencom Corporation, of New Orleans, Louisiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstruction of infant's breathing, violating the federal crib bumper ban.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the crib bumpers immediately and request a full refund. Those who purchased in-store at MacroBaby in Orlando, Florida, can return the item for a refund. Online purchasers should email info@kalencom.com with the subject line 'Crib Bumper Recall - Refund Request,' including their name, phone number, and a photo of the crib bumper cut in half as proof of destruction.

These collapsible walkers feature a round base, eight wheels, and seven adjustable height settings, and were available in gray, black, and pink. The production batch number '7654' can be found on a tracking label beneath the seat.

recalls.Description:
Little Pea Shop has issued a recall for two styles of their crib bumpers due to serious safety concerns. The 'Waffle Bedding Accessory' bumpers, available in gray, pink, blue, and white, were sold in lengths of 23.5 inches, 47 inches, 78.5 inches, and 94.5 inches. Additionally, the 'Plush Crib Bumper' style, designed as vertical slat covers with Velcro, comes in beige and ivory featuring bear, rabbit, or star designs. These products pose a suffocation risk, violating federal regulations intended to keep infants safe during sleep.

Parents and caregivers are advised to cease use of these crib bumpers immediately. The padded design can obstruct an infant's breathing, creating a hazardous sleeping environment. Compliance with the Safe Sleep for Babies Act is crucial to ensure the safety of infants.
recalls.Title: Recall of Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Hazard; Noncompliance with Federal Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Little Pea Shop via email at support@littlepeashop.com, or visit https://littlepeashop.com/pages/recall or https://littlepeashop.com/ and select 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 2025 through October 2025 for between $8 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Little Pea Shop, of France

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstruction of an infant's breathing, violating federal crib bumper regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the crib bumpers, cut the padding, and send a photo of the destroyed bumpers to Little Pea Shop for a full refund.

recalls.Description:
Sofoliana and Glotika baby loungers are being recalled due to significant safety concerns. These loungers feature a rectangular design with a foam sleeping pad and padded bumpers, but they do not meet safety standards for infant sleep products. The Sofoliana model is dark gray with a ribbon tie, while the Glotika model zips into a bag with an adjustable strap and is available in gray-rocket, pink-heart, and white-elephant designs. Both models lack proper labeling or markings.

The loungers present serious risks, including inadequate side height that fails to contain infants and excessively wide openings at the foot, leading to potential falls and entrapment. Additionally, the absence of a stand increases the risk of falls when used on elevated surfaces. These hazards create an unsafe sleeping environment, which could result in severe injury or even death.
recalls.Title: Recall of Sofoliana and Glotika Baby Loungers Due to Entrapment and Fall Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, please contact Bosen US at BosenRecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sofoliana Baby Lounger
Recalled Glotika Baby Lounger in white-elephant
Recalled Glotika Baby Lounger in grey-rocket
Recalled Glotika Baby Lounger in pink-heart

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2025 through October 2025. Sofoliana baby loungers were sold for between $30 and $40. Glotika baby loungers were sold for between $40 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Bosen Trading Co., Ltd., dba Bosen US, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and fall hazards due to low sides and wide openings, violating infant sleep product safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungers immediately and contact Bosen US for a full refund. Destroy the loungers by cutting the cover, foam, and pad in half, and send photos of the destroyed items to BosenRecall@outlook.com to receive your refund.

recalls.Description:
Alinux baby loungers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns, including risks of entrapment and falls that could lead to severe injury or death. These loungers feature a foam sleeping pad, padded bumpers, and a cloth cover with a ribbon tie. They were available in pink, grey, and purple colors. The model number 'BP25001' and batch number 'ZZ-FBA25052305-1' can be found on a label attached to the cover of the product.

The loungers do not meet the mandatory safety standards for infant sleep products, as their sides are too low to adequately contain an infant, and the openings at the foot are wider than permitted. Additionally, the lack of a stand increases the risk of falls when placed on elevated surfaces, creating an unsafe sleeping environment for infants.

These swings, available in white/gray and black/gray, are marked with the "VEVOR" trademark and labeled as "Multi-Function Electric Swing" along with their model numbers. The white/gray swings with a white base are identified as model BB501K, while those with a gray base are model BB702A. The black/gray swings feature model number BB005.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recalling@vevor.com, or visit https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-baby-swing-recall. Additional information can be found by clicking "Product Recall" on the Vevor website.

Umeyda is recalling its children's short-sleeve nightgowns made from 100% cotton due to a serious fire hazard. These nightgowns, available in various colors including black, purple, and mint green, are sold in sizes ranging from 4-5T to 13-14 years. Each gown features a pocket and buttons down the front, with identifying details printed on a side-seam label.

These products do not meet the required flammability standards for children's sleepwear, which increases the risk of burn injuries. Parents and guardians should take immediate action to ensure the safety of their children by discontinuing use of these nightgowns.
recalls.Title: Recall of Umeyda Children's Nightgowns Due to Fire Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Umeyda Company via email at ameyda123@163.com.

These high chairs, which can transform into various seating options, including a toddler armchair and a rocking chair, are at risk of causing injury or death due to falls and entrapment. The affected chairs are identifiable by the model number BHC001 and production batch number 202408, both located on a label on the back of the chair. recalls.Description:
Belivium has issued a recall for their baby loungers due to serious safety concerns, including risks of suffocation and entrapment. These loungers consist of a foam sleeping pad and padded bumpers covered in fabric, available in various designs such as yellow zoo animals, blue dinosaurs, and pink butterflies. Each product features a label stating "BABY NEST" inside the cover. The loungers do not meet safety standards, which can create dangerous sleeping conditions for infants.

The loungers have been flagged for having inadequate side heights and excessive sleeping pad thickness, leading to potential suffocation hazards. Additionally, the openings at the foot of the loungers are too wide, increasing the risk of falls and entrapment. Due to these violations, the loungers present a significant risk of severe injury or death to infants, prompting immediate action from consumers.
recalls.Title: Belivium Baby Loungers Recalled Due to Suffocation and Entrapment Risks; Noncompliance with Infant Sleep Product Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Belivium via email at BeliviumRecall@163.com.

recalls.Description:
LXDHSTRA has issued a recall for their baby loungers, which feature a foam sleeping pad and padded bumpers with fabric covers. These loungers were available in various designs, including tan star, big elephant, green elephant, gray feather, and gray rainbow, and included a crib bumper with letters "A" through "F" on one side and animal graphics on the other. The products pose multiple safety hazards that could lead to severe injury or death for infants.  
The loungers do not meet federal safety standards for infant sleep products, as their sides are too low, and the sleeping pad is excessively thick, creating a suffocation risk. Additionally, the openings at the foot of the loungers are wider than permitted, leading to potential falls and entrapment. The absence of a stand further increases the risk of falls when used on elevated surfaces. Furthermore, the inclusion of crib bumpers contravenes the Federal Safe Sleep for Babies Act, which bans such items due to their suffocation risks.
recalls.Title: Recall of LXDHSTRA Baby Loungers and Crib Bumpers Due to Serious Suffocation and Fall Risks; Noncompliance with Federal Infant Sleep Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to LXDHSTRA via email at lxdhstrarecall@gmail.com.

recalls.Description: Youbeien-branded crib mobiles are being recalled due to a serious risk of injury or death from battery ingestion. These mobiles, featuring a timing function for music and lights, are available in pink and purple. They include a plastic arm that attaches to the crib, a music box, and four dangling animal characters such as a penguin, pig, lion, and unicorn. The mobile measures approximately 17 inches wide by 20 inches high and comes with a remote control for music and rotation. The packaging displays the brand name 'Youbeien' and model 'RT668-17' on a label at the back.
recalls.Title: Recall of Youbeien Crib Mobiles Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard; Sold by GKKBSJ on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact GKKBSJ via email at gkkbsjtoys@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: URMYWO baby loungers in the 'grey feather' design are being recalled due to safety concerns. These loungers feature a gray cloth cover adorned with feather prints and include a foam sleeping pad along with padded bumpers. Labels on the side of the loungers display 'URMYWO' and 'Model No: UMCZC01AE'.
recalls.Title: Recall of URMYWO Baby Loungers Due to Suffocation and Fall Risks; Violates Infant Sleep Product Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Pomona via email at urmyworecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled URMYWO Baby Lounger – Grey Feather
Tracking label located on side of recalled baby lounger
Label with “URMYWO” brand name on side of recalled baby lounger

Retailers.Name: Amazon from January 2024 through April 2025 for between $30 and $50.

Distributors.Name: Dong Guan Bo Mo Na Ke Ji You Xian Gong Si, of China, doing business as Pomona

Hazards.Name: The loungers do not meet mandatory safety standards for Infant Sleep Products, as their sides are shorter than required, the sleeping pad is too thick, creating a suffocation risk, and infants may fall out or get trapped. Additionally, the lack of a stand increases the fall hazard, making these loungers unsafe for infant use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the loungers immediately and reach out to Pomona for a full refund. To process the refund, remove the sleeping pad, cut the sides of the loungers and the pad, and email a photo of the destroyed product to urmyworecall@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: Lulive is recalling its 12-drawer dressers due to potential hazards that could lead to serious injuries or fatalities from tip-over incidents. These dressers, featuring a metal frame and wooden top, come in white and include 12 fabric drawers, four side pockets, and two hooks. They measure approximately 12 inches by 39 inches by 45 inches and weigh around 30 pounds. The product packaging displays the code "KF-X9Y1".

recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers should contact Lulive at 866-610-4348, available from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT Monday through Friday. They can also email lulive12recall@sina.com or visit www.lulive.com/recalls. For additional details, click on the "Recall" section at the top of the homepage.

These toys, which come with six pre-installed button cell batteries, include a laser pointer that measures approximately 2.9 inches long and features blue paw prints. The LED ball is clear, about 1.6 inches in diameter, and is activated by motion. Both products are packaged with the 'PET ZONE' logo and specific item details printed on the packaging.

recalls.Description:
Innovative Designs is recalling Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelets, specifically Models 718508MCR and 718075MCR, available in pink and green. These bracelets feature the 'MINECRAFT' logo on the front packaging and have model numbers printed on the back. Each bracelet contains three pre-installed button cell batteries, posing a serious safety risk to children. 
The battery compartments of these bracelets can be easily accessed by young children, creating a risk of ingestion. If a child swallows a button cell or coin battery, it can lead to severe injuries, internal chemical burns, or even death. It is crucial for parents to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Innovative Designs Recalls Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelets Due to Button Cell Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email info@innovativenyc.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelet, Model 718508MCR - Front

Recalled Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelet, Model 718075MCR - Back

Injuries.Name: Innovative Designs has received one report of a battery separating from the toy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores nationwide from March 2025 through May 2025 for about $2.

Importers.Name: Innovative Designs LLC, of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion of button cell batteries due to easily accessible battery compartments.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bracelets immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Innovative Designs for disposal instructions and a full refund. To receive the refund, email a photograph of the disposed item to info@innovativenyc.com. Dispose of button cell batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.Description: Diamond G Forest Products is recalling its 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine, packaged in 4-ounce clear glass bottles. These round bottles feature labels that read 'Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine' and 'Diamond G Forest Products.' The recall is prompted by the absence of child-resistant packaging, which is a requirement under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. This oversight poses a significant risk of serious injury or even death if young children accidentally ingest the contents.
recalls.Title: Diamond G Forest Products Issues Recall for Turpentine Bottles Due to Child Poisoning Hazard

Consumers are urged to check these details to see if their product is affected by the recall.

recalls.Description:
Primark is recalling its DTR Stitch Scented Highlighters 4-pack, which includes highlighters in yellow, blue, pink, and green, designed to look like slushie cups with straws. Each highlighter features a Stitch character, and the Kimball Number for this product is 0688701, found on the price tag at the back of the packaging. This recall is due to the presence of phthalates, which violate federal regulations and pose health risks, particularly to young children if ingested.

Consumers are urged to stop using these highlighters immediately and return them to any Primark location for a full refund using the original payment method. Ensuring the safety of children is a priority, and this action is necessary to mitigate any potential health risks associated with these products.
recalls.Title: Primark Recalls DTR Stitch 4-Pack Scented Highlighters Due to Phthalate Exposure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Primark at 617-946-3236 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.primark.com/recall or www.primark.com and click 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Each bumper features a care label with specific letters and numbers stitched onto it. The product does not comply with federal crib bumper regulations, which aim to protect infants from potential breathing obstructions.

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled crib bumpers immediately and reach out to YIXIA for a full refund by emailing Yixiakejirecall@163.com.

The recall highlights a violation of the Federal Safe Sleep for Babies Act, which prohibits padded crib bumpers.

recalls.Description: WLIVE has issued a recall for its Fabric 15-Drawer Dressers due to significant safety risks. These dressers, available in rustic brown, black, charcoal black wood grain, and white, can become unstable if not securely anchored to a wall, posing a serious tip-over and entrapment hazard, particularly for children. The dimensions of the dresser are approximately 12 inches long, 40 inches wide, and 51 inches tall, with a weight of around 36 pounds. The model number "ASNG105" can be found on the packaging box.
recalls.Title: WLIVE Fabric 15-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Risks; Violations of Federal Safety Standards

recalls.Description:
The recall pertains to Creekliybear "Pack and Play" Aftermarket Play Yard Mattresses, which are white with gray stripes. These mattresses are labeled with "Play Mat" and "Model: m-sy2" and measure approximately 26 inches in width and 38 inches in length. Due to their excessive thickness, these mattresses do not meet mandatory crib mattress standards and may not fit properly in some play yards, leading to a serious entrapment hazard for infants.

Infants can suffocate if they become trapped between the mattress and the side walls of the play yard, especially if their face is pressed against the mattress. This poses a significant risk of injury or death, making it crucial for consumers to act immediately.
recalls.Title: Recall of Creekliybear Play Yard Mattresses Due to Entrapment Risk; Non-Compliant with Crib Mattress Standards

recalls.Description:
Peg Perego is recalling its Tatamia 3-in-1 recliners, swings, and high chairs due to a potential suffocation risk. These versatile products can be used as a recliner, high chair, and unpowered infant swing. The affected items are available in an 'Ice' (gray) color and are identified by the model number IMTATANA01BL73. Only those manufactured on or after June 23, 2022, or purchased after November 12, 2022, are included in this recall. Consumers can find the model number and manufacture date on the product's base.

The recall is prompted by safety concerns as these products have an incline greater than 10 degrees, which violates the Infant Sleep Products Rule and the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Additionally, they do not meet the required warnings under high chair standards, posing further risks to infants.
recalls.Title: Peg Perego Recalls Tatamia 3-in-1 Recliners, Swings, and High Chairs Due to Suffocation Hazard and Federal Standard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Peg Perego at 877-737-3464 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email service@pegperego.com, or visit https://www.pegperego.com/en_us/baby/recalls for more information.

recalls.Description: Boyro Baby is recalling its baby walkers, model US-B01, due to significant safety risks. These plastic walkers, available in black, blue, and gray, feature five height adjustments, a rotating seat, six wheels, and four removable toys. The product is marked with "Ready To Ride" on one of the legs, while the tracking label with the brand name and model number is located under the seat cushion. The design allows these walkers to fit through standard doorways and they do not comply with safety standards regarding fall prevention, posing a serious risk of injury or death to children. Additionally, the brake pads contain lead levels that exceed federal safety regulations, which is hazardous to young children.
recalls.Title: Boyro Baby Walkers Recalled Due to Fall Hazard and Lead Content Violations; Sold Exclusively on Amazon.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Boyro Baby via email at boyrobaby@outlook.com or visit https://www.boyrobaby.com/round-walker-safety-recall/ or www.boyrobaby.com and click on "Round Walker Safety Recall".

recalls.Description:
TADAKAZU is recalling its baby loungers due to significant safety concerns that could lead to serious injury or death. These loungers, featuring a cloth cover, foam sleeping pad, and padded bumpers, have been identified as violating federal safety standards for infant sleep products. The loungers, marketed as 'baby nests,' have various prints and model numbers, including styles like Beige, Grey-leaf, Pink, and more. The brand name 'TADAKAZU' is displayed on the bumpers of each product.

The safety violations include inadequate side height and excessive sleeping pad thickness, posing suffocation hazards and risks of falls or entrapment. Parents and caregivers are urged to discontinue use immediately to ensure the safety of their infants.

These high chairs stand approximately 30 inches tall, 21 inches wide, and 23 inches long, and were available in various colors including blue, black, natural wood/white, warm wood/gray, and white. Each chair features an item code located on the bottom of the seat for identification.

recalls.Description: Funlio has issued a recall for their 2-in-1 Baby High Chairs, which can transform into a chair and table for growing children. Constructed from natural wood, these high chairs feature a removable padded seat insert with a green forest design. The brand name "Funlio" is displayed on the front of the high chair and on a tracking label located beneath the tray, which also includes the model number "FL-039."
recalls.Title: Recall of Funlio Convertible High Chairs Due to Fall and Entrapment Risks; Federal Standards Violated
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ecogoods at 800-251-8805 from 5:30 p.m. to 1:30 a.m. PT Sunday through Thursday, via email at funlioproductrecall@outlook.com, or visit https://funliotoys.com/pages/product-recalls or https://funliotoys.com and click on "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Balejiao E-commerce Co. Ltd., d/b/a Ecogoods, of China

Consumers will need to cut the restraint straps and send photos of the disassembled and destroyed parts to funlioproductrecall@outlook.com. Ecogoods will directly notify all known purchasers.

recalls.Description: Ravmix has issued a recall for their portable hook-on chairs designed for young children. These chairs feature a black metal frame with a polyester and cotton covering in black or gray, along with a white plastic tray for meals. The design includes two metal arms that secure the chair to a dining table, allowing the child to be suspended above the surface. The product is referred to as a 'Children's table chair' and is identified by model number 'S006' in the owner's manual.
recalls.Title: Ravmix Portable Hook-On Chairs Recalled Due to Fall Hazard Risk and Federal Standard Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, please email Ravmix at ravmix@outlook.com.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to removable crotch restraint, violating federal safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the chair and contact Ravmix for a refund process, which includes disassembling the chair and sending photos of the parts.

recalls.Description: The recall affects ZHORANGE portable bassinets identified by model number 148012-A. These bassinets are gray, feature a head pillow, and can be folded into a handbag with a handle, displaying the brand name 'ZHORANGE' prominently. The interior design includes black polka dots, and a tag indicating the model number and manufacturing date in YYYY.MM format is present. The bassinets do not meet federal safety standards for infant sleep products, as their sides are too low to safely contain infants and lack a stand, creating a fall hazard.
recalls.Title: Recall of ZHORANGE Portable Bassinets Due to Fall Hazard Risk and Safety Standard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to DFD Trading via email at zhorange8695@gmail.com.

recalls.Description: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball sets, model number 711, have been recalled due to a serious choking hazard. These sets feature a base with buttons, a detachable door, four small basins, and a monkey sign. The green base is labeled "FOREST ROLLER BALL," while the brown gate displays "ROLLER BALL," and the hammer reads "FISHING." The dimensions of the toy are 15 x 10.6 x 13.2 inches, and it includes a hammer along with four small colored balls, each measuring approximately 1.5 inches in diameter, available in yellow, light pink, dark pink, and blue. The model number can be found on the product packaging label.
recalls.Title: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball Sets Recalled Due to Choking Hazard Risk; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, please email haochenghengkj@outlook.com or visit www.hcfjehtoy.com or www.amazon.com/stores/page/2912E5E1-628F-4384-930C-5D8CCB7B8184.

recalls.Description: ZRWD is recalling its infant swings, model S0008A, due to a significant suffocation hazard. These swings, available in gray and white or blue and white fabric, feature a black or white base, a remote control with a lithium CR 2025 3v button cell battery, two plush toys (moon and star), and an activity tray. The swings have an incline greater than 10 degrees, violating safety standards intended for infant sleep products.
recalls.Title: ZRWD Infant Swings Recalled Due to Suffocation Risk and Safety Violations; Sold Exclusively on Amazon.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to ZRWD via email at zrwdrecall@163.com.

recalls.Description: Step2 is recalling its My First Snowman toy due to a choking hazard. The toy features a removable plastic top hat and comes with detachable facial features and mittens. Standing approximately 17 inches tall, the snowman is white with purple mittens, blue ears, and distinctive colored eyes and buttons. Only units manufactured in December 2023 and April 2024 are affected, with the production date code located on the bottom of the hat near the battery compartment.
recalls.Title: Step2 Recalls My First Snowman Toys Due to Choking Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled snowman, remove the hat, and keep it away from children. For further instructions on disposing of the hat and obtaining a free replacement, contact The Step2 Company at 800-347-8372 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, via email at step2-company@step2.net, or visit https://www.step2.com/pages/step2-my-first-snowman or www.step2.com and click on 'Product Recalls' for more details.

recalls.Description: Playworld Systems is recalling its Unity Teeter Tunnels, identified by part numbers ZZXX0182, ZZXX0182S, ZZXX0192, and ZZXX0192S. These seesaws are designed for multiple children to stand, sit, or lie down, and they feature a tunnel for crawling. The part numbers can be found on the original purchase order as well as on the installation instructions.
recalls.Title: Playworld Systems Recalls Unity Teeter Tunnels Due to Serious Injury and Crush Hazard Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Playworld Systems at 800-233-8404 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recalls@playworld.com or visit https://playworld.com/recall-information for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Unity Teeter Tunnel with Lexan Top (first model)
Recalled Unity Teeter Tunnel with Lexan Top (second model)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of children falling under the end of the seesaw plank of the recalled Unity Teeter Tunnels. One child sustained a liver laceration when the end of the seesaw plank landed on her abdomen and the second child suffered fractures to his right tibia and right fibula when the end of the seesaw plank landed on him.

Manufacturers.Name: Playworld Systems Inc., of Lewisburg, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Playworld Systems sales representatives and their authorized independent sales representatives nationwide from October 2014 through March 2025 for between $13,450 and $13,600.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury and crush hazard if a child falls underneath the seesaw.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the teeter tunnels immediately and contact Playworld Systems for a free repair, which will be conducted by a certified playground installer.

recalls.Description: Macy's has announced a recall of five styles of Holiday Lane-branded infant footed pajamas due to a potential injury hazard. These cotton pajamas are available in infant sizes 0-3 months, 3-6 months, and 6-9 months, featuring 'Holiday Lane' on the neck label. The size and PID/Style numbers can be found on the care instruction tag located on the side seam of the pajamas. The affected styles include Merry Mix It, Family Plaid, Winterton Plaid, Star, and Floral designs.
recalls.Title: Macy's Issues Recall for Holiday Lane Infant Footed Pajamas Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled pajamas, keep them away from children, and reach out to Macy's at 833-963-1708 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email footedpajamas@realtimeresults.net. More information is available online at https://www.recallrtr.com/footedpajamas or by visiting www.macys.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Holiday Lane Infant Merry Mix It Cotton Matching Family Footed Pajama

Images.Caption: Recalled Holiday Lane infant footed pajamas
PID/Style number location

Injuries.Name: Macy's has received four reports of the elastic being too tight on infants' ankles, resulting in chafing, imprinting or restricting blood circulation.

Retailers.Name: Macy's stores nationwide, including Macy's Backstage, and online at www.macys.com from March 2024 through January 2025 for about $27.

Importers.Name: Macy's Merchandising Group Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Elastic on the pajamas' ankles may be too tight, risking blood circulation and causing injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pajamas, keep them away from children, and contact Macy's for a refund. Do not return the pajamas to stores. A recall form can be filled out at https://www.recallrtr.com/footedpajamas, after which a pre-addressed, postage-paid envelope will be provided for returning the pajamas at no cost. A refund of $26.50 will be issued via check upon receipt of the returned product. Macy's will directly contact all known purchasers.

recalls.Description:
Nezmdi is recalling its electric infant swings due to serious safety concerns, including a suffocation hazard that could lead to injury or death. These swings, designed for infant sleep, have an incline greater than 10 degrees, violating the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Additionally, they do not comply with essential warning requirements and the included remote control lacks necessary safety instructions regarding lithium coin batteries.

The swings feature a pink design with a white base, a black control panel, plush toys, a pillow, a seat cover, and a mosquito net canopy. They measure approximately 25.5 inches by 25.5 inches by 20.8 inches and come with a remote and USB cable. A label under the seat displays the model number "S0008" and the product name "Baby Swing Chair."
recalls.Title: Nezmdi Electric Infant Swings Recalled Due to Suffocation Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nezmdi via email at yingpengds@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nezmdi infant swing
A leather patch with the brand Nezmdi located on restraints
"Model Number: S0008" and "Baby Swing Chair" are printed on a label located under the seat
Recalled Nezmdi infant swing remote control

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from September 2024 through February 2025 for about $70.

Importers.Name: Ningboyingpengdianzishangwuyouxiangongsi/YingPengStore, dba Nezmdi, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard and risk of serious injury or death due to incline angle and lack of required warnings.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the swings immediately and request a full refund from Nezmdi by sending a photo of the cut swing cover marked 'Recalled' to yingpengds@163.com. Dispose of the swing according to local regulations.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for various child safety gates sold by multiple third-party sellers on Amazon.com. These gates, made from steel and ABS plastic, come in black and white colors and can be either pressure-mounted or drill-mounted. They feature model numbers and cohort information on their tracking label located on the bottom rail. Some models also include a secondary pet door, which adds to the risk of entrapment and falls. The affected gates vary in size and include several configurations.
recalls.Title: Child Safety Gates Recalled Due to Entrapment and Fall Hazards; Compliance Issues Identified by Yiwu Baili Import and Export
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to the third-party seller via email for details on product disposal and to request a full refund. Specific email addresses for each seller are provided in the product details.

recalls.Description: Fat Brain Toy Co. is recalling individual PlayTab Rollers Tile toys that are part of the PlayTab Modular Activity Board. These toys, which feature colorful rubberized rollers designed for children to touch and spin, were sold separately or as part of the PlayTab Sensory Set 3. The recall is due to a serious hazard where a magnet can detach, posing a risk of ingestion that may lead to severe injury or even death.
recalls.Title: Fat Brain Issues Recall for PlayTab Rollers Tile Toys Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Fat Brain Toy Co. at 800-590-5987 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@fatbraintoys.com, or visit https://fatbraintoys.com/info/toy_safety/play_tab_roller_tile_recall.cfm. You can also find details by clicking "Recall" on the top of fatbraintoys.com.

Importers.Name: Fat Brain Toy Co. LLC, of Elkhorn, Nebraska

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to loose magnets, which can lead to severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys and contact Fat Brain for disposal instructions to receive a $10 credit or a replacement tile.

recalls.Description: Boyro Baby has issued a recall for their high chairs due to serious safety concerns. These 4-in-1 convertible high chairs, available in green, gray, and pink, feature a removable seat cushion, detachable tray, footrest, and under-seat storage. They can be transformed into a low chair, rocking chair, or slide, but pose significant hazards to children. The restraint system is improperly attached, and the design allows for potential entrapment between the seat and tray, violating federal safety regulations.
recalls.Title: Boyro Baby High Chairs Recalled Due to Fall and Entrapment Risks; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Boyro Baby via email at boyrobaby@outlook.com or visit www.boyrobaby.com/testimonial or www.boyrobaby.com and select "Safety Recalls".

Images.Caption: Recalled Boyro Baby convertible high chair

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com and from October 2023 through November 2024 for between $70 and $90.

Distributors.Name: Boyro Baby, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall and entrapment hazards due to improper restraint system attachment and large openings between the seat and tray.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled high chairs immediately, disassemble them, and contact Boyro Baby for a full refund. Instructions will include cutting the restraint system and seat cushions, and sending photos of the disassembled parts to boyrobaby@outlook.com. A refund will be processed upon receipt of the photos.

recalls.Description: Deuter has issued a recall for its Kid Comfort, Kid Comfort Pro, and Kid Comfort Active Child Carriers due to a potential fall hazard. These carriers feature a backpack design with a foldable frame equipped with hinges. They were available in maroon and green forest (model 3621221), blue midnight (model 3621321), and teal (model 3621121). The brand name 'deuter' along with the product name can be found printed on the back of each carrier. A production code is located on a tag inside the main storage pocket, with affected codes ending in 0123, 1223, or 0423.
recalls.Title: Deuter Recalls Kid Comfort, Kid Comfort Pro, and Kid Comfort Active Child Carriers Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled child carriers immediately and reach out to Deuter for a free replacement. Contact Deuter at 877-384-9252 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email recall@deuterusa.com, or visit https://www.deuter.com/us-en/service/kid-comfort-recall for further assistance.

recalls.Description: Xylolin peg doll sets are being recalled due to a serious choking hazard that could lead to injury or death. These sets include nine colorful peg dolls of varying sizes and a natural wood-colored base designed to hold the dolls. The product packaging features the model number "SD13" on a white label. It is important for parents and caregivers to be aware of this recall to ensure the safety of their children.

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Babywants via email at recall@twoodtoy.com or visit www.xylolintoys.com/pages/recall-page for further assistance.

recalls.Description: Eonroacoo has issued a recall for COMEONROA electric infant swings due to serious safety concerns, including a risk of suffocation. These swings, featuring a blue space-themed design, are equipped with a black base, touch-screen panel, plush toys, and a mosquito net canopy. They measure approximately 25.5 inches in length, 25.5 inches in width, and 20.8 inches in height, and come with a remote control and USB cable. The model number 'S-Y608' is found on a label beneath the seat.

recalls.Description: Costway has announced a recall of the Babyjoy 6-in-1 Convertible High Chair Activity Centers due to serious safety concerns. These products, available in beige (model BB5693YW) and gray (model BB5693GR), feature a removable adjustable tray, a cushioned seat, adjustable-height legs, and a mesh storage pocket. The seat padding in the activity center mode is white with multicolored dots, and 'Babyjoy' is labeled on the tray, while 'Goplus Corp.' appears on a tag at the back of the seat. The model number can be found on the product packaging.

recalls.Description: Bebamour is recalling its baby bath seats, identified by model number 'T186' on the tracking label located behind the backrest. These bath seats measure approximately 14 inches in length, 12 inches in width, and 9 inches in height. Available only in green, each seat features four large suction cups and three bath toys, made from polypropylene and thermoplastic elastomer materials. The front displays the 'Bebamour' brand, and the handle includes a button that sprays water, complemented by star designs on the seat.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Bebamour via email at amanda@babyleading.com or visit https://bebamour.com/pages/recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to https://bebamour.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

recalls.Description: Fisher-Price has issued a recall for the tissue box toy included with the 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers. The model number for the activity center is HLV78, which can be found on the bottom of the table near the Fisher-Price logo. The toy is white with red accents and features sensory cloth 'tissues' with a black and white wavy striped design on one side and a yellow-green side with raised spots.
recalls.Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Tissue Box Toy from 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx or www.service.mattel.com and click on 'Recalls & Safety Alerts' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Tissue Box Toy from Fisher-Price 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,300 (In addition, about 2,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tissue Box Toy in SnugaPuppy Activity Center
Recalled Tissue Box Toy

Injuries.Name: Fisher-Price has received one report of the tissue box toy coming apart and an infant placing the small support bracket in their mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Walmart stores nationwide, and online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com and other websites from November 2022 through February 2025 for about $130.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price Inc., of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Detachable tissue box toy may separate, revealing small parts that pose a choking hazard to young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled tissue box toy immediately, remove it from the activity center, keep it away from children, and contact Fisher-Price for a free replacement. They will need to permanently mark the toy with 'Recall' and a unique identifier, mark 'X' on the sensory cloth tissues, and upload a photo of the marked toy at https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx. After receiving confirmation for the replacement, consumers should dispose of the recalled item. Detailed marking and photo upload instructions can be found at https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx. Fisher-Price is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: HONEYJOY COMPANY is recalling several models of high chairs made from metal and plastic, featuring cloth seats in various colors and prints. These high chairs are being recalled due to a suffocation hazard, as they were improperly marketed for infant sleep and have an incline angle exceeding 10 degrees, which violates federal safety regulations. The affected models include the HONEY JOY 3-in-1 Baby Swings, HONEY JOY High Chair with Wheels, HONEY JOY Convertible Foldable Highchair, HONEY JOY 5-in-1 High Chair, and others, all identifiable by their model numbers found on the top cover of the seat.
recalls.Title: HONEYJOY Recalls Convertible and Foldable High Chairs Due to Suffocation Risk and Regulatory Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to HONEYJOY via email at honeyjoyrecall@hotmail.com or visit their Amazon store at https://www.amazon.com/stores/page/A401562A-3E1E-43A1-8F23-3AAE28BA14FC?ingress=2&visitId=596d5ffc-9ec0-4624-baae-6aad8d0c70dd&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&ref_=ast_bln. For further details, click "MORE" at the top of the page and select "RECALL HIGH CHAIR" from the dropdown menu.

recalls.Description: Superb Sports has issued a recall for their baby bath seats due to serious safety concerns. These seats, available only in light blue, feature a built-in anti-slip cushion and are equipped with four large suction cups for stability. Made from polypropylene (PP) and ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA) plastic, they display playful stickers of clouds, a lion, a sun, and a squirrel with balloons. The dimensions of the bath seats are approximately 12 inches in length, 13 inches in width, and 8 inches in height.
