recalls.Description: Five Below has announced a recall of its Room2Room LED iridescent bear lights due to potential fire and burn hazards. These lights measure approximately 7.5 inches in height and 9.3 inches in width, featuring a USB plug and an on/off switch. The product is identifiable by the labels "Room 2 Room," "LED Iridescent Bear Light," and the model number "LD-0030" located on a sticker at the base of the bear. The UPC code for this item is 1922340368444, found on the packaging.
recalls.Title: Five Below Issues Recall for Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Lights Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Five Below at 844-452-3569, available from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional support can be obtained via email at Recalls@FiveBelow.com or by visiting https://www.fivebelow.com/info/five-below-recalls-led-iridescent-bear-light. Consumers can also find more details by clicking on the "Product Recalls" section at the bottom of the Five Below website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Light
Location of label under the recalled Room2Room LED Iridescent Bear Light

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 28 reports of the USB cord overheating and melting, including six reports of burns to fingers. There were also two reports of property damage, including burns to a wall and a headboard.

Retailers.Name: Five Below stores nationwide and online at fivebelow.com from September 2024 through February 2025 for about $12.

Distributors.Name: 1616 Holdings Inc., of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Five Below Inc., of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Overheating power switch on USB cord leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights immediately and return them to any Five Below store for a full refund, or contact customer service for a refund by submitting a photo of the light with the cord cut.

recalls.Description: LaRose Industries has announced a recall for ten models of Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem jewelry kits, including one refill pack. These kits feature a resin bottle and a UV light for curing the resin, packaged in rectangular boxes marked with the "Cra-Z-Art" brand and either "Gemex" or "crazy gel2gem." The item numbers and UPC codes are located on the bottom of the product boxes, indicating the specific models affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: LaRose Industries Issues Recall for Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem Jewelry Kits Due to Health Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the jewelry kits and reach out to LaRose Industries for a full refund. For assistance, contact LaRose toll-free at 877-770-7029 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email gemgel@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/gemgel or https://cra-z-art.com/ and click "Recall" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Cra-Z-Art Gemex Shimmer N Sparkle Gel Set

Retailers.Name: Target, Marshall's, T.J. Maxx, and Joann stores, and other retailers nationwide and online at Amazon.com, Target.com, Walmart.com and Joann.com from January 2023 through September 2024 for between $13 and $36, depending on the kit.

Importers.Name: LaRose Industries LLC, dba Cra-Z-Art, of Randolph, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: The resin in the jewelry-making kits can cause skin, eye, and respiratory irritation or sensitization when inhaled, touched, or ingested. It contains an acrylate (hydroxyethylmethacrylate "HEMA") in amounts that violate the Federal Hazardous Substances Act. Consumers should inspect any created jewelry to ensure it is fully cured; if any liquid or residue remains, the jewelry should not be worn and should be kept away from children. Once fully cured, the resin is no longer hazardous.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the jewelry kits right away and contact LaRose Industries for a complete refund. LaRose is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: Onasti toddler stools are being recalled due to significant fall and injury hazards. These foldable, triangular stools come in various colors including beige/white, blue/gray, green, gray/white, light blue, and pink. They measure approximately 25 inches in length, 24 inches in width, and 32 inches in height, featuring handles and an adjustable platform that can be configured for two or three steps. The Onasti logo is displayed on the warning label located on the side of the stools.
recalls.Title: Recall of Onasti Toddler Tower Stools Due to Fall and Injury Risks; Available Exclusively on Amazon.com by Blissful-Time
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Blissful-Time at 833-999-9327, email support@onasti.net, or visit https://onasti.com/pages/product-recall-information or https://onasti.com/ and select 'Product Recall Information'.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiyaxinrankejiyouxiangongsi, dba Blissful-Time, of China

recalls.Description: Chews Life has issued a recall for its Silicone Rosary and Decade teething toys due to a potential choking hazard. The affected products include the Silicone Rosary models: Assisi, Little Flower, God's Grace, Cecilia, John the Baptist, and Divine Mercy, as well as the Silicone Decade models: Genesis, Maccabees, Ester, Wisdom, Divine Mercy, Psalm, and Proverbs. Each teether features colorful beads and a cross, along with a two-piece safety snap clasp that may detach.
recalls.Title: Chews Life Recalls Silicone Rosary and Decade Teething Toys Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Chews Life at 888-742-2276 from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email sandy@chewslife.com, or visit https://chewslife.com/pages/clasp-recall or www.chewslife.com and click on 'Recall Notices' for further details.

Recalled Decade teethers

Replacement safety snap clasp on repaired teethers

Retailers.Name: Online at chewslife.com from January 2024 through April 2024 for between $20 and $38.

Distributors.Name: Chews Life, of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Detachable part of the safety snap clasp posing a choking hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled teethers immediately and contact Chews Life for a free repair. A prepaid shipping label will be provided to return the items, and repaired teethers will be sent back at no cost.

recalls.Description:
HabiLife Direct has issued a recall for their retractable safety mesh gates, identified by model number SG021. These gates, available in white, gray, and black, are designed for use as baby or dog gates. Standing approximately 34 inches tall and adjustable up to 55 inches wide, they feature an aluminum frame with the model number and 'Retractable Safety Gate' printed on a label inside the frame near the handle. The design poses a serious entrapment hazard, violating federal safety regulations as a child's torso can fit through the gap between the gate and the floor.

Consumers are urged to stop using these gates immediately and contact HabiLife Direct for a full refund. As part of the remedy, consumers will need to destroy the gates by cutting the mesh and disposing of them, and must provide a photo of the destroyed gates to service@hblife.fans. HabiLife Direct and Amazon are proactively reaching out to all known purchasers regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: HabiLife Direct Recalls Retractable Safety Gates Due to Entrapment Risk for Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact HabiLife Direct at 771-232-2236 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email service@hblife.fans.

Recalled HabiLife safety gate - gray

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from September 2024 through January 2025 for between $35 and $45.

Distributors.Name: Yiwushihengchuansanyeyouxiangongsi, dba HabiLife Direct, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to design flaw allowing child's torso to fit through the gap

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates, destroy them, and send a photo of the destroyed gates for a refund

Parents should be aware of the dangers these products pose to infants.

recalls.Description:
Marie-Chantal has issued a recall for various styles of children's nightgowns, pajama sets, and robes due to noncompliance with federal flammability standards. These garments, made from 100% organic cotton or 100% pima cotton, include nightgowns available in multiple prints and colors, as well as pajama sets and bathrobes in various designs. Sizes range from 2 years to 10 years for nightgowns and pajamas, while robes are available in small, medium, and large.

The nightgowns feature designs such as birds, bunnies, and flowers, while pajama sets come in styles with kittens and angel wings. The robes are offered in combinations of white and pink or blue. All items are marked with the brand name "Marie-Chantal" and RN number 103490 on a sewn-in label.
recalls.Title: Recall of Marie-Chantal Children's Nightgowns, Pajama Sets, and Robes Due to Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to immediately stop using the recalled garments and keep them away from children. For a full refund, contact Marie-Chantal at 888-824-0940 from 8 a.m. to 1:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@mariechantal.com. More information is available at https://www.mariechantal.com/pages/product-recalls or by visiting www.mariechantal.com and clicking on "Recall" at the top of the page.

These fans can easily wrap around stroller bars and are identifiable by their black color and the Nuby brand logo on the front. The lot number N8K10X and model number 25138 can be found on a white label at the back center of the fan.

recalls.Description:
Forever 21 has issued a recall for 20 styles of children's pajamas due to a serious burn hazard, as they do not meet federal flammability standards. The affected pajamas come in various styles including pants, shorts, and two-piece sets, available in children's sizes ranging from 5/6 to 13/14. Each item features a label with the size and 'Forever 21' branding, along with a specific item number on a side-seam label below the barcode.

Consumers are urged to stop using these pajamas immediately and reach out to Forever 21 for a full refund. The company will provide instructions for destroying the pajamas, which includes cutting them in half and sending a photo of the destroyed items to the provided email address. Forever 21 is also directly contacting all known purchasers regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Forever 21 Recalls Children's Pajamas for Burn Hazard Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Forever 21 at 888-219-4074 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Sunday, email recall@forever21.com, or visit www.forever21.com/recall.

Distributors.Name: F21 OpCo LLC, dba Forever 21, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to violation of flammability regulations

recalls.Description: Nazzaro is recalling their bowling pin sipper cups, identified as Rebecca's Toys & Prizes. These cups are primarily white with red tape around the neck and feature a red plastic cap. A white label on the side displays the item number "PG1045", along with the brand name and company address. This recall is due to the presence of lead in the red tape, which exceeds federal safety limits. Lead poses serious health risks if ingested, particularly for young children.
recalls.Title: Nazzaro Issues Recall for Bowling Pin Sipper Cups Due to Lead Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Nazzaro at 800-777-2235 between 10 a.m. and 5:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email service@rebeccas.com or visit https://www.rebeccas.com/recall.html for more details.

recalls.Description:
SHEIN is recalling its EVRYDAY Kids pajama sets made of 97% polyester and 3% elastane due to safety concerns. These two-piece, short-sleeve pajamas, featuring a pocket on the left side of the top, were available in blue, pink, purple, and orange. Sizes include 8Y, 9Y, 10Y, 11Y, and 12Y, with 'SHEIN' and the size printed on the neck label and the SKU number on a seam label.  
The pajamas do not meet federal flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, which poses a burn hazard to young children.

recalls.Description: The 6-in-1 Pounding Game, featuring a wooden frame with a frog clock, xylophone, colorful gears, and animal face pegs, has been recalled due to safety concerns. This toy includes wooden carrots and worms with metal heads, along with two mallets and a bird that contains a strong magnet, which poses an ingestion hazard if it detaches. The risk arises when children swallow the magnet, potentially leading to severe internal injuries.

recalls.Description:
UVIPC Baby Gates are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These pressure-mounted gates can open both inward and outward, made from steel and ABS plastic, and are available in white and black. There are four versions of the gate, with heights around 40.5 inches and varying adjustable widths. Some versions include a secondary pet door, which creates a risk of entrapment for children. The gates are labeled with model number W046 on their tracking label.

Consumers are urged to stop using these gates immediately. The design flaws violate federal safety standards for expansion gates and enclosures, posing a potential entrapment hazard. Xianshengyouli and Amazon are actively reaching out to all known buyers to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: Recall of UVIPC Baby Gates Due to Entrapment Risk and Federal Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Xianshengyouli at 626-529-6048 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or email xianshengyouli@outlook.com.

Each set contains between three and twelve pieces of jewelry featuring designs such as unicorns, butterflies, hearts, turtles, stars, and rainbows. The items are silver-colored with multicolored gems and are packaged in small plastic bags within a lavender box labeled "A Special Gift for the Charming You." The name "Yaomiao" is also visible on the box's plastic wrap.

This product features three handrails and seating for up to six children, with a platform that is level with the surrounding ground. The affected model numbers are 560-0042 and 560-0051. Owners are encouraged to reach out to BCI Burke to verify whether their unit is part of the recall.

recalls.Description: ONME Direct has issued a recall for GIKPAL 12-drawer dressers due to serious safety concerns. These dressers, available in rustic brown, black, or white finishes, pose a risk of tipping over if not securely anchored to a wall. The model numbers affected are HI1437_13, HI1437_14, and HI1437_02. Each dresser measures approximately 31 inches wide, 12 inches deep, and 48 inches tall, weighing around 35 lbs. The packaging indicates the model number and states 'Made in China.'

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact ONME Direct at 833-449-5777 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT on weekdays, and from 1 p.m. to 2 p.m. PT on weekends. You can also reach them via email at Greenservice88@outlook.com or visit https://onmeus.com/pages/recall for more details.

Each spoon measures approximately six inches in length. The recall affects all spoons with date codes 2024-01 for three-packs and 2023-12 to 2024-05 for four-packs, as well as all spoons included in the Baby 5-Piece Silicone Feeding Sets.

recalls.Description:
Stokke has announced a recall for YOYO³ strollers with serial numbers beginning with '142AA' and followed by numbers less than '0062239.' Only these specific strollers are impacted. Designed for children from birth to 48 months, the strollers feature a compact foldable design and come in various colors including black, Bonpoint beige, stone, olive, taupe, toffee, ginger, Air France blue, and aqua. The serial number can be found on a label located beneath the stroller seat.

The recall stems from a safety concern where the parking brake may disengage, leading to a risk of injury. Consumers are urged to stop using these strollers immediately and visit the official recall website for further instructions.

recalls.Description:
Petidoux is recalling its Celebration Girl's Summer Pajama Sets due to a serious burn hazard. These pajamas, made from 100% cotton, feature a white base with a colorful firework print and are available in various sizes ranging from 1 to 12. The design includes blue trim and a small bow on the collar, with care instructions and safety warnings printed inside the neck area. Unfortunately, these pajamas do not meet federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear, which poses a risk of burn injuries to young children.

Consumers are advised to immediately stop using these pajamas and keep them away from children. Petidoux is offering a full refund for the recalled items. To process the refund, consumers must destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half and disposing of them according to local recycling guidelines. A photo of the destroyed garment must be sent to customerservice@petidoux.com to complete the refund process or to receive a store credit equivalent to 1.25 times the purchase price.
recalls.Title: Petidoux Recalls Celebration Girl's Summer Pajama Sets Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Petidoux at 888-381-7171 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email customerservice@petidoux.com, or visit https://www.petidoux.com/pages/cpsc-recall for more details.

recalls.Description: Baofali is recalling its 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Safety Bumpers due to a significant suffocation hazard. Each bumper measures approximately 52 inches in length and 10 inches in height. These products do not comply with the federal Safe Sleep for Babies Act, which prohibits padded crib bumpers.

recalls.Description: Dreamgro is recalling its lullaby travel soothers designed for infants, which are shaped like mermaids and come with a soft gold star attached to the head. Available in light blue and pink, these soothers play music and feature a detachable hook for easy attachment to car seats and strollers. The Dreamgro brand name and model numbers can be found on the tag of the soothers. The affected models include the Light Blue Mermaid (DF156-MT) and Pink Mermaid (DF156-LI).
recalls.Title: Dreamgro Recalls Mermaid Lullaby Travel Soothers Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Dreamgro at 888-202-0442 from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email Recall@dreamgro.com. Visit www.dreamgro.com/recall.html or www.dreamgro.com and select 'Product Recall,' or reach out to the retailer where the product was purchased.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dreamgro Lullaby Travel Soothers (blue)
Recalled Dreamgro Lullaby Travel Soothers (pink)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the soft star detaching from the toy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Beall's Outlet Stores, Burlington Merchandising, DD'S Discounts, Macy's Backstage, Marshalls, Nordstrom Rack, and TJ Maxx stores nationwide from January 2024 through May 2024 for between $6 and $9.

Importers.Name: DreamGro Enterprises LLC, of New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to detachable gold star

Remedies.Name: Stop using the soothers and return them for a full refund

recalls.Description: Sanven Technology has announced a recall of Vevor Baby Gates due to a significant entrapment hazard that violates federal safety standards for gates and enclosures. These gates, identifiable by the markings "Vevor, Tough Tools Half Price" on the handle, include five screws and four wall cups. Designed to be pressure-mounted, they can swing both inward and outward and are constructed from powder-coated iron and plastic, available in white and black. The gates stand approximately 30 inches tall and can be adjusted to widths ranging from 29.5 inches to 39 inches.
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for Vevor Baby Gates Due to Entrapment Risk and Safety Regulation Noncompliance
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Sanven Technology at 888-559-1726 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at csr@vevor.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vevor baby gate and accessories

Retailers.Name: eBay.com, AliExpress.com, and Overstock.com from February 2024 through May 2024 for about $45.

Importers.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to violation of federal safety regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gates immediately and contact Sanven Technology for disposal instructions and refund processing.

recalls.Description:
Fisher-Price has announced a recall of the Snuga Swing, an infant swing that moves both front to back and side to side. This swing features a 90-degree rotation, two adjustable seat back positions, and is designed for easy storage. The recall affects 21 different models, which share a similar design but vary slightly in fabric colors and toy accessories. The model number can be found on the underside of the swing's motor housing.

Parents and caregivers should be aware that the Snuga Swing is not intended for sleep, and adding bedding materials can increase the risk of suffocation. A complete list of affected models is available, and consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Over 2 Million Snuga Infant Swings Due to Serious Suffocation Risk Linked to 5 Fatalities
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.service.mattel.com and select 'Recall & Safety Alerts' or go to www.service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx for detailed information.

Products.Name: All Models of Fisher-Price Snuga Swings
Products.NumberOfUnits: Approximately 2.1 million swings were sold in the United States, about 99,000 swings were sold in Canada and about 500 swings were sold in Mexico

Images.Caption: Recalled My Little Snugabunny Cradle 'n Swing (V0099)

Recalled My Little SnugaMonkey™ Cradle 'n Swing (X7051)

Recalled My Little Snugabear Cradle 'n Swing (X7347)
Representative recalled Snuga Swing headrest
Representative recalled Snuga Swing body support insert

Injuries.Name: Between 2012 and 2022, there have been reports of five deaths involving infants 1 to 3 months of age when the product was used for sleep. In most of those incidents, the infants were unrestrained and bedding materials were added to the product.

Retailers.Name: The Snuga Swings were sold at Amazon, Toys R Us, Walmart/Sams Club, and Target nationwide from October 2010 through January 2024 for about $160.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper use for sleep and added bedding materials.

Remedies.Name: Remove the headrest and body support insert; Fisher-Price offers a $25 refund for compliance.

recalls.Description:
Gimars Playpens, identified by model numbers GWL-01 through GWL-06, are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These playpens, which feature the name "Gimars Playpen with mat" on a label attached to the top rail, come in dark gray, black, and light gray fabrics with white mesh sides. They include a reversible mattress adorned with road and animal designs, as well as a basketball hoop and multicolored balls that pose risks to children. The brand name "Gimars" is printed in white on one side of the top rail.

The playpens are being recalled because they do not meet federal safety regulations for play yards. The basketball hoop accessory can create a head entrapment risk, and children may become trapped beneath the mattress, leading to potential suffocation hazards. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Gimars Playpens Recalled for Entrapment and Suffocation Risks; Non-compliance with Federal Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Shenzhen Jingdu Technology via email at Gimars-Aftersales@outlook.com, or visit https://gimarsrecall.com/ or https://gimarsbaby.com and select "Safety Recalls" for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gimars Playpen
Recalled Gimars Playpen basketball hoop accessory
Recalled Gimars Playpen reversible mattress
Recalled Gimars Playpen reversible mattress
Recalled Gimars Playpen label

Retailers.Name: Online exclusively atwww.Amazon.com from May 2023 through January 2024 for about $80.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Jingdu Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of federal safety regulations; head entrapment and suffocation hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the playpen, disassemble it, cut the fabric floor, mattress pad, and basketball hoop into two pieces, and submit photos for a refund.

recalls.Description:
Wee Gallery has announced a recall for the Baby Tummy Time Gallery due to safety violations regarding phthalate levels. This product features a trifold design made of black and white fabric, equipped with clear plastic pockets intended for art cards. It was available for purchase individually or as part of a bundle with the art cards. The gallery is identifiable by a hang tag that includes the phrases "wee gallery," "tummy time gallery," and "wildlife."

The recall is prompted by the discovery that the clear plastic coverings on the art card pockets contain phthalate levels that exceed federal safety standards. Phthalates are harmful substances that can pose serious health risks if ingested by young children. Parents and caregivers are urged to take immediate action regarding this product.
recalls.Title: Wee Gallery Issues Recall for Baby Tummy Time Gallery Over Phthalate Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Wee Gallery at 800-282-5149 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at tummytimerecall@weegallery.com, or visit weegallery.com/tummytimerecall. More details can also be found by navigating to the "Recalls" section at the bottom of the weegallery.com homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wee Gallery Tummy Time Gallery – Partially Opened
Recalled Wee Gallery Tummy Time Gallery – Opened

Retailers.Name: Independent toy stores nationwide and online at weegallery.com, amazon.com, bloomingdales.com and babylist.com from October 2022 through July 2024 for about $38, when sold separately, and approximately $54 when sold as part of a bundle.

Importers.Name: Wee Gallery Inc., of St. Petersburg, Florida

Hazards.Name: The Baby Tummy Time Gallery contains clear plastic pockets with phthalates that exceed federal safety limits, posing health risks to young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the Baby Tummy Time Gallery immediately, contact Wee Gallery for disposal instructions, and request a refund of $39.95. Wee Gallery and Amazon.com will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: Newmemo has issued a recall for their children's ring sets due to hazardous levels of lead and cadmium. These sets, which contain 36 rings in various designs, were packaged in a pink heart-shaped display case. The product is identifiable by the name "Newmemo" and the Amazon FNSKU X0034COQMP found on a label on the case's underside.
recalls.Title: Newmemo Children's Ring Sets Recalled for Lead and Cadmium Hazard; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled rings immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Newmemo for a full refund. recalls.Description: Shawshank LEDz has issued a recall for its Plush Ball Monster Squeezable and Easter Squeezable toys due to safety concerns. The Monster Squeezable toys are identified by model number 702053 and UPC 810447020536, while the Easter Squeezable toys have model number 768152 and UPC 810067681520. Both identifiers can be found on a removable hang tag, with lot numbers PC: 0224 for Monsters and PC: 1223 for Easter toys displayed on the sewn tag.
recalls.Title: Shawshank LEDz Recalls Plush Ball Monster and Easter Squeezable Toys Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Shawshank LEDz Inc. at 480-802-1085 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email robert@shawshankledz.com or visit https://shawshankledz.com/Disclosures-recall.html for more details.

The loungers feature a tag stating 'SLEEPING SET' on the interior and often include the brand name on the exterior, although some may lack these identifiers.

recalls.Description: RH Baby & Child is recalling their Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Cribs due to a potential choking risk. These cribs, identifiable by SKU numbers 113210, 113313, and 113754, measure approximately 30 inches in width, 55½ inches in length, and 42 inches in height. Available in brushed gray oak, brushed light oak, and brushed white oak finishes, the cribs feature upholstered end and lower panels, three adjustable mattress height settings, and can be converted into toddler beds. SKU numbers can be found on the purchase receipt and on a label on the inner-lower section of the upholstered panel.
recalls.Title: RH Baby & Child Recalls Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Cribs Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach RH at 833-611-1775 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, and 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. They can also email recall@rh.com or visit https://rhbabyandchild.rh.com/us/en/customer-experience/safety-recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Crib
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 480 (In addition, about 10 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled RH Baby & Child's Jeune French Contemporary Upholstered Panel Crib
The SKU numbers are printed on the receipt, and on a label located on the inner-lower portion of the upholstered panel

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of a metal insert loosening and detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: RH Baby & Child stores, RH outlets nationwide, and online at https://rhbabyandchild.rh.com from January 2021 through March 2024 for between $600 and $2,900.

Importers.Name: RH US LLC, of Corte Madera, California

Hazards.Name: Loose cylindrical metal inserts in the crib's frame may detach, creating a choking hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled cribs immediately and contact RH for a full refund or a replacement crib with free shipping. If a receipt is not available, a $1,260 cash refund or credit will be issued, or a new replacement crib can be provided at no cost. RH will also arrange for free pickup of the recalled crib and is reaching out to all known purchasers.

These vehicles are identifiable by the "Peace Sports" branding on the handlebar pad and sides, with model numbers found on the VIN plate located on the lower front frame. A notice stating the ATV is part of an action plan approved by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission is also present on the vehicle.

recalls.Description:
Beberoad is recalling its New Moon Travel Bassinets due to a fall hazard that violates federal safety regulations for infant sleep products. These bassinets, available in various colors including dark gray, blue, light gray, pink, purple, and sky blue, feature a folding design with a mesh mosquito net. The model name 'New Moon' is visible on the canopy, and the mattresses are adorned with a star pattern. Only bassinets manufactured on or after June 23, 2022, are affected by this recall, with the model number TB999-1 found on a sewn-in tag at the bottom of the bassinet.

Consumers are urged to stop using these bassinets immediately and reach out to Beberoadlove for guidance on obtaining a full refund and proper disposal methods. Beberoadlove and Amazon are proactively contacting all known purchasers regarding this issue.
recalls.Title: Beberoad Love New Moon Travel Bassinets Recalled for Fall Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Beberoadlove at 334-316-0066 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email service@beberoad.com, or visit https://beberoad.com/pages/new-moon-travel-bassinets-safety-recall or https://beberoad.com/ and click on 'NEWMOON TRAVEL BASSINET RECALL' at the top of the page.

recalls.Description:
Stiger has announced a recall for the Besrey Twins Strollers, Model BR-C708S, due to significant safety concerns. These strollers come in gray and black fabric with black frames and wheels, featuring canopies that can be positioned up or down. The front grab bar may be covered in either black or light gray fabric, with the model number and manufacturer details located on a white label near the back right wheel. The instructional literature refers to the model as BR-708S.

The strollers are found to violate several safety regulations, presenting risks such as entrapment, falls, and choking hazards. Specifically, the front seat configuration poses an entrapment risk, while issues with the restraint system and rear seat enclosure can lead to falls. Additionally, the foam padding on the black grab bar may pose a choking hazard if bitten by a child.
recalls.Title: Stiger Issues Recall for Besrey Twins Strollers Due to Safety Risks Including Entrapment, Fall, and Choking Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to cease using the stroller immediately and reach out to Stiger for guidance on product disposal and to secure a full refund. For assistance, contact Besrey at 888-310-6255 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@besrey.com or stigerstg@outlook.com. More information can also be found online at https://www.besrey.com/pages/product-recalls-safety-alerts or by visiting www.besrey.com and clicking on "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the homepage.

recalls.Description:
Fashion Online has issued a recall for children's two-piece pajama sets available in eight distinct styles: I LOVE DAD, I LOVE MOM, TUTU AWAKE TO SLEEP, LOL Bright Pink, LOL Light Pink, Unicorns Forming a Heart, Standing Unicorn, and Unicorn in Heart. These pajamas, sized from 1Y to 8Y, feature a side-seam label indicating '100% Cotton', 'Made in China', along with size and washing instructions, but lack a neck label. Each style has unique designs and colors aimed at children.

The recalled pajama sets do not meet federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear, which poses a significant burn hazard. Parents and guardians are urged to take immediate action to ensure the safety of their children by ceasing use of these garments and following the outlined remedy steps to receive a refund.

recalls.Description:
Joybuy Marketplace Express has issued a recall for sling carriers intended for carrying infants. These carriers feature an adjustable shoulder strap, a black plastic buckle, and a hook-and-loop waist and crotch restraint, with a solid navy exterior and various interior colors. Notably, the brand name is not displayed on the products, so consumers are urged to check their purchase history to identify if their sling carrier is affected by this recall. Affected models were sold on Walmart.com through various sellers, including Wisremt and Joybuy Express.

The recalled sling carriers do not meet the required safety standards for structural integrity and occupant retention, which can lead to fall hazards. Additionally, the lack of proper restraint systems creates a suffocation risk, as the waist restraint can be used independently of the crotch restraint. There are also no warnings or instructions included to guide caregivers on safe positioning to prevent suffocation risks for infants.
recalls.Title: Recall of Sling Carriers Due to Infant Suffocation and Fall Risks; Non-Compliance with Federal Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Joybuy Marketplace Express at 302-426-4543 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, via email at oversea-service@jd.com, or online at https://www.walmart.com/seller/16214.

recalls.Description: SIORO is expanding its recall of 100% cotton terry cloth children's robes due to safety concerns. These long-sleeved robes feature a hood, two front pockets, and a matching belt. The additional colors included in this recall are Rose Pink, Light Pink, Pure White, Falcon Gray, and Green Mist, alongside previously recalled colors such as Light Blue, Navy, Peacock Blue, Gray Blue, and Plum. Available sizes range from 4-5T, 6-7 Years, to 8-10 Years, with labels indicating '100% Cotton' and 'Made in China' on the neck.
recalls.Title: SIORO Children's Robes Recall: Burn Hazard and Flammability Standards Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, email amazonus@sioro.com or visit the SIORO Amazon store at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=AUPGGE9NR4SBV and select 'Ask a question'.

Products.Name: SIORO Children's Robes
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,000 (About 950 children's robes were previously recalled in June 2021)

Images.Caption: Recalled Rose Pink Children's Robe
Recalled Light Pink Children's Robe
Recalled Pure White Children's Robe
Recalled Falcon Gray Children's Robe
Recalled Green Mist Children's Robe
Recalled Light Blue Children's Robe
Recalled Navy Children's Robe
Recalled Peacock Blue Children's Robe
Recalled Gray Blue Children's Robe
Recalled Plum Children's Robe

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from November 2021 through July 2023 for between $20 and $27.

Importers.Name: SIORO, of China

Hazards.Name: Violation of flammability standards for children's sleepwear, increasing the risk of burn injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must remove the recalled robes from children, cease using them, and contact SIORO for a full refund. Garments should be destroyed by cutting them in half, and a photo of the destroyed item should be sent to amazonus@sioro.com. Upon receiving the photo, SIORO will issue a refund. Finally, dispose of the destroyed robes in accordance with local recycling regulations.

recalls.Description: Cotton On USA has recalled its promotional toy pinwheels due to a potential choking hazard. These multi-colored pinwheels, featuring the words 'KIDS' and 'COTTON ON' in white lettering, were distributed for free at Cotton On stores. The fastener cap on the pinwheels may become loose and detach, posing a risk to young children.
recalls.Title: Cotton On USA Issues Recall for Toy Pinwheels Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Cotton On USA at their toll-free number 844-319-5970, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to customerenquiries@cottonon.com or through their website at cottonon.com/US/product-recall.html. More details can also be found by visiting www.cottonon.com/US and clicking on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pinwheel

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the fastener cap coming loose and being swallowed by a child.

Retailers.Name: Given away as promotional items at Cotton On Kids Stores in Scottsdale, Arizona; Christiana, Delaware; Willowbrook, Texas; Florida Mall, Orlando, Florida; Cool Springs, Tennessee, and Mall of Louisiana, Franklin, Louisiana, from April 2023 through January 2024 for free.

Importers.Name: Cotton On USA Inc., of La Mirada, California

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to loose fastener cap

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the pinwheels and dispose of them. No refunds or replacements will be issued as the items were free.

seo.metaDescription: Cotton On USA recalls toy pinwheels over choking hazard. Learn how to dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: Dixon Ticonderoga is recalling its Creativity Street Foam Pattern Rollers, model PAC5170, designed for children's painting activities. The recall affects rollers with lot codes '02142080423' and '02142230523', which can be found on the back of the packaging. These foam rollers feature handles in yellow, green, blue, and red and come in various patterns.
recalls.Title: Dixon Ticonderoga Recalls Creativity Street Foam Pattern Rollers for Lead Content Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Dixon Ticonderoga at 800-333-2545 from 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email compliance@dixonusa.com, or visit https://dixonticonderogacompany.com/creativity-street-foam-pattern-rollers/ or www.dixonticonderogacompany.com and click 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Importers.Name: Dixon Ticonderoga Company, of Appleton, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Lead content exceeds federal safety standards, posing health risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the rollers immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Dixon Ticonderoga for a return label and full refund.

recalls.Description: Rooms To Go has announced a recall of the Mill Valley Jr. six-drawer youth dressers, available in white and cherry finishes. These dressers, measuring approximately 57.5 inches in length, 18 inches in width, and 35 inches in height, can pose serious safety risks if not anchored to the wall, leading to potential tip-over and entrapment hazards for children. The model numbers for these dressers are C2192Y-N04 for the white version and C2193Y-N04 for the cherry version, identifiable by a label on the back panel.
recalls.Title: Recall of Mill Valley Jr. Youth Dressers from Rooms To Go Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Rooms To Go at 855-688-0919, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, or via email at productcare@roomstogo.com. More details can be found online at www.MillValleyJuniorRecall.com.

recalls.Description:
SKIMS has issued a recall for its fleece children's pajama sets due to a serious burn hazard. These two-piece, button-up pajamas feature long sleeves and wide-legged pants, available in various plaid designs including blue and black, multi-colored, green and black (cypress), and burgundy and black (wine). Sizes range from 2T to 10, and labels indicate 'SKIMS', the size, and 'Made in Philippines'.

The pajama sets do not comply with federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a risk of burn injuries. Parents and guardians are urged to stop using these pajamas immediately and take necessary precautions to ensure children's safety.
recalls.Title: SKIMS Children's Pajama Sets Recalled for Burn Hazard Due to Flammability Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, please contact Skims Body at 877-206-1172 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email pajamasets@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/pajamasets or www.skims.com and click 'Product Recall' for more details.

Products.Name: SKIMS Fleece Children's Pajama Sets
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,200 (In addition, 23 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Skims Body Pajama Set – Black and Burgundy Plaid

Retailers.Name: Online at skims.com and in-store at the SKIMS store in Austin, Texas, from November 2023 through January 2024 for about $54.

Importers.Name: Skims Body Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Flammability violation posing burn injury risk

Remedies.Name: Return for full refund and destroy garments

recalls.Description: Igloo is recalling its 12 oz. Youth Sipper Bottles, which feature the brand name "IGLOO" on the side. These stainless-steel bottles come with a silicone cover on the sipper and were available in various color combinations, including pink and purple, blue and green, and others. The recall is due to a potential choking hazard for children if the silicone cover detaches during use.
recalls.Title: Igloo Products Issues Recall for Youth Sipper Bottles Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Igloo at 800-273-7024, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, email customerservice@shop.igloocoolers.com, or visit www.igloocoolers.com/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Igloo 12 oz. Youth Sipper Bottle
Recalled Igloo 12 oz. Youth Sipper Bottle with the cap off

Injuries.Name: The firm reported one incident of the silicon sleeve coming off. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Academy Sports + Outdoors, Rural King, and online at www.igloocoolers.com from April 2023 through March 2024 for between $13 and $17.

Importers.Name: Igloo Products Corp., of Katy, Texas

Hazards.Name: Detachable silicone cover poses choking risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, destroy the silicone spout, and contact Igloo for a replacement or refund

recalls.Description: Crate & Barrel is recalling Hampshire Cribs due to a potential fall hazard. These cribs were available in various colors including blush, olive, white, gray, and natural brown. Each crib features one of the following SKU numbers: 246898, 534758, 534987, 535020, and 405394, with the brand name "Crate&Barrel" displayed on a label on the mattress support board.
recalls.Title: Crate & Barrel Recalls Hampshire Cribs Over Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Crate & Barrel at 800-967-6696 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, or 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT on weekends. More information can be found online at www.crateandbarrel.com/customer-service/product-recalls or www.crateandkids.com by clicking on the "Recalls" link at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Crate & Barrel Hampshire Cribs

recalls.Description: Sant and Abel has issued a recall for two styles of children's pajama sets made from 100% cotton. These sets, available in sizes ranging from 1-2 to 9-10, feature neck labels indicating the brand, size, and fabric content. The side seam labels provide washing instructions along with the origin of manufacture, either 'MADE IN CHINA' or 'MADE IN INDIA'. The long-sleeve Henley and pant combinations come in various prints such as Kids' Sky Blue Stripe and Peony Stripe, while the two-piece sets are offered in designs like Kids' Leaf and Gingham.
recalls.Title: Sant and Abel Recalls Children's Pajama Sets Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Sant and Abel at 213-328-2448 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, email returns@santandabel.com, or visit www.santandabel.com/pages/us-product-recall. Alternatively, visit www.santandabel.com and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Sant and Abel Children's Cotton Pajama Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Sant and Abel Children's Henley Two-Piece Pajama Sets (Sky Blue Stripe, Peony Stripe, Red Stripe, Navy Stripe, Wreath, Horse, Christmas, Candy Cane and Daisy)

Recalled Sant and Abel Children's Traditional Collared Two-Piece Pajama Sets (Boys' White Cambric, Girls' White Cambric, Girls' Billie Neon, Gingham Pink, Gingham Green, Gingham Blue, Palm Tree Blue, Palm Tree Pink, Palm Tree Green, Banana Leaf Pink, Banana Leaf Green, Banana Leaf Blue and Houndstooth)

Retailers.Name: Online at santandabel.com, neimanmarcus.com, maisonette.com, SAKS.com and Flip.com from January 2021 through February 2024 for about $59.

Importers.Name: Sant and Abel, of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet flammability standards for children's sleepwear, creating a burn risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the pajamas immediately, remove them from children, and contact Sant and Abel for a full refund. They should destroy the pajamas by cutting them in half and disposing of them according to local recycling laws. A photo of the destroyed item should be sent to returns@santandabel.com to receive the refund. Sant and Abel will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.Description:
Gasaciods is recalling their children's multi-purpose helmets designed for head circumferences of approximately 19-1/2 to 20-1/2 inches. Available in pink and blue, these helmets feature red removable padding, black straps, and a black and red buckle. A notable design element is the red plastic knob at the back, equipped with an LED warning light for fitting adjustments, with "GASACIODS" printed on the sides.

These helmets do not meet crucial safety standards regarding stability, retention system strength, and impact protection, as mandated by CPSC regulations for bicycle helmets. This non-compliance increases the risk of head injuries during accidents, making their use unsafe for children.
recalls.Title: Gasaciods Children's Helmets Recalled for Head Injury Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled helmets immediately and reach out to Fengwang Sports for a refund. Contact them via email at george66305@163.com or through the message feature on their Temu seller profile.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gasaciods Children's Multi-Purpose Helmet (Blue - front view)
Recalled Gasaciods Children's Multi-Purpose Helmet (Blue - side view)
Recalled Gasaciods Children's Multi-Purpose Helmet (Pink- rear view)

Retailers.Name: Online at Temu.com from July 2023 through January 2024 for between $23 and $42.

Importers.Name: Dongguan Feng Wang Sports Goods Co., Ltd. DBA Fengwang Sports, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with safety regulations increases the risk of head injury.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets, destroy them, and contact Fengwang Sports for a refund.

recalls.Description:
Huenco has issued a recall for BRS portable liquid fuel bottles, which are available in orange with a black cap and come in sizes of 530mL, 750mL, and 1000mL. Each bottle features 'BRS', its volume, and safety warnings in white lettering. These bottles do not comply with child-resistant closure requirements, posing serious burn and poisoning hazards to children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these bottles immediately and ensure they are kept out of children's reach. For disposal instructions and to obtain a full refund, please contact Huenco directly.
recalls.Title: Recall of BRS Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Poisoning and Burn Risks; Non-Compliance with Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act
recalls.ConsumerContact: Email at chencaiyu1994@hotmail.com, or visit their Amazon seller profile at Amazon.com Seller Profile: Huenco.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements leading to burn and poisoning risks for children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, keep them away from children, and contact Huenco for disposal instructions and a full refund.

recalls.Description: Yoto is recalling the Yoto Mini, a portable speaker designed for children aged three to twelve. This device, which operates using Yoto cards, allows users to enjoy audiobooks, music, radio, and podcasts. The Yoto Mini features a pale gray body with orange controls and a pixel display. The product's model name, Yoto Mini, along with SKU PRPLXX00860, can be found on its base. The recalled units were sold with a charging cable but did not include an adapter or charger.
recalls.Title: Yoto Mini Speakers for Kids Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yoto Ltd. at 844-370-0426 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at minihelp@yotoplay.com, or visit https://yotoplay.com/recall. Alternatively, go to https://us.yotoplay.com/ and click on "RECALL INFORMATION" at the top for further details.

Products.Name: Yoto Mini Speakers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 251,165 (In addition, about 18,932 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Yoto Mini
Base of Recalled Yoto Mini

Injuries.Name: Yoto has received six reports from U.S. consumers and one from a UK consumer of the speaker overheating or melting. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at us.yotoplay.com, Amazon.com, Target.com and maisonette.com, and in toy and gift stores nationwide from November 2021 through April 2024 for about $70.

Importers.Name: Yoto Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery posing burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the speakers, keep them away from children, and contact Yoto for a free replacement charging cable. Send a photo of the old cable cut in half to receive the new cord.

recalls.Description: Zazaba International has announced a recall of their Grão de Gente baby nests, which consist of a cloth cover, foam sleeping pad, and padded bumper. These products, available in various colors and designs, are identified by the label 'Grão de gente' sewn onto the cover. The recall is due to safety concerns that pose risks of suffocation, falls, and entrapment for infants. Specific issues include inadequate side height, excessive sleeping pad thickness, and potential openings that could allow infants to fall or become trapped.
recalls.Title: Zazaba International Issues Recall for Grão de Gente Baby Nests Due to Safety Hazards and Violations of Federal Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Zazaba International Inc. at 855-425-4640 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at recall@graodegente.com, or visit https://graodegente.com/pages/baby-nest-recall. Additional information can be found by clicking 'Recall Notices' on their website.

recalls.Description: Lovey & Grink has announced a recall of several types of children's sleepwear, including girl's two-piece pajama sets, nightgowns, boy's two-piece pajama sets, and unisex two-piece pajama sets. These garments were sold in various fun prints such as Sushi, S'mores, and Rainbow Hearts, and are available in sizes ranging from 12 Months to 9/10. Important labels on the neck include 'Made In Indonesia', 'RN64664', and material composition details.
recalls.Title: Lovey & Grink Recalls Children's Pajamas Due to Fire Hazard Violating Safety Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Lovey & Grink at 877-360-5470, via email at recall@loveyandgrink.com, or visit www.loveyandgrink.com/pages/product-safety. More details are also available on their website under the 'Product Safety' section.

recalls.Description: Thule is recalling its RideAlong rear-mounted Child Bike Seats due to potential chemical ingestion risks. These seats, designed to fit most bicycle frames, are available in gray and feature a black 3-point harness padding. The affected model number is 100107, and the recall includes date codes from March 2021 to October 2023, which can be found on the underside of the seat.
recalls.Title: Thule Recalls RideAlong Child Bike Seats Over Chemical Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Thule at 888-816-0228, available from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email thuleridealong.americas@thule.com or visit www.thule.com/recallthuleridealongamer for more details. Additional information is accessible on their website under the "Product Support" section by selecting "Product Recall."

recalls.Description:
Forias is recalling its pack and play mattresses, which are portable memory foam mattresses measuring approximately 38 inches by 26 inches. These mattresses have tags on the underside of the cover and a stitched label featuring the brand name 'Forias'. The recall is due to safety violations related to crib mattress standards, posing a suffocation hazard for infants.  
Consumers are urged to stop using these mattresses immediately and properly dispose of them by cutting them in half. To receive a full refund, customers should send a photo of the destroyed mattress to customer.care@forias.com. Both Amazon and Forias Direct are reaching out to all known buyers directly to provide assistance.
recalls.Title: Forias Pack and Play Mattresses Recalled Due to Infant Suffocation Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Forias Direct at customer.care@forias.com or visit https://forias.com/pages/recall-information. You can also find details by clicking 'Recall Information' under 'HELP&SUPPORT' at the bottom of the homepage.

recalls.Description: Stateside Bead Supply is recalling its 5mm High-Powered Magnetic Beads due to a significant ingestion hazard. These small, spherical magnets exhibit a strong magnetic force and were sold in strands within clear plastic packaging labeled with the company name. The beads are gun metal gray and pose a risk to children due to their ability to attract each other or metal objects if swallowed, potentially leading to severe health issues.
recalls.Title: Stateside Bead Supply Recalls High-Powered 5mm Magnetic Beads Due to Serious Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, contact Stateside Bead Supply via email at info@statesidebeadsupply.com or call their toll-free number at 888-644-3448, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT. More details can also be found at https://www.statesidebeadsupply.com/recall-notice.html or by visiting www.statesidebeadsupply.com and selecting 'Recall Notice.'

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Beads HIGH POWER 5mm AAA Grade
Recalled Magnetic Beads HIGH POWER 5mm AAA Grade (Magnetic Beads are sold on a strand in a plastic bag)

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths since 2005 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Manufacturers.Name: Boss Magnet, of Taiwan

Retailers.Name: Online at www.statesidebeadsupply.com from November 2022 through June 2023 for between $7 and $9.

Distributors.Name: Stateside Bead Supply Inc., of Issaquah, Washington

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to strong magnets that can cause serious digestive injuries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the beads, keep them away from children, and contact Stateside Bead Supply for a pre-paid return label to receive a full refund.

recalls.Description: Fisher-Price has issued a recall for the plastic Donald Duck and Daisy Duck figures included in the Little People Mickey and Friends figure pack, models HPJ88 and HTW75. Each figure stands approximately 3 inches tall, featuring Donald in a blue shirt with yellow stripes and a red bow tie, while Daisy sports a light purple shirt and pink accessories. The model number can be found on the bottom of the Goofy figure in the pack.
recalls.Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Little People Mickey and Friends Figures Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More details are available online at www.service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx or www.service.mattel.com under 'Recalls & Safety Alerts'. You can also reach them via email at https://consumersupport.mattel.com/mattelsupport/s/create-case.

recalls.Description: Twenty Four Six Foods has announced a recall of all flavors of its Happiness USA liquid rolling candy, which includes blue raspberry, strawberry, and green apple varieties. Each bottle contains approximately one fluid ounce of flavored liquid candy and features a rolling ball applicator designed for easy application onto the tongue. The product is packaged in a resealable bottle with a twist-off plastic cap, and is labeled with "Funtime Roller Ball Candy" on the front and "Happiness USA" branding along with warning labels on the back.
recalls.Title: Twenty Four Six Foods Issues Recall for Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the candy immediately, keep it away from children, and reach out to Twenty Four Six Foods for a full refund. For assistance, contact them toll-free at 833-436-1200 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, or via email at recall246foods@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (front)
Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (back)
Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (all flavors)

Injuries.Name: Twenty Four Six Foods has received one report of the rolling ball dislodging, resulting in a seven-year-old child choking in New Jersey in July 2023. The child underwent emergency surgery to successfully remove the rolling ball from his throat.

Retailers.Name: Kosher market stores nationwide from January 2018 through July 2023 for about $2.

Importers.Name: Twenty Four Six Foods LLC, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to dislodging rolling ball

Remedies.Name: Stop using the candy and contact for a refund

recalls.Description: Jool Baby is recalling its Nova Baby infant swings, which were produced between June 2022 and September 2023. These swings, featuring a gray design, measure approximately 28 inches long, 19 inches wide, and 24 inches high. They include a round aluminum base with music buttons, a metal seat frame, a cloth seat with safety restraints and headrest, and a canopy adorned with hanging toys shaped like a yellow moon, blue cloud, and pink star. The swings can operate on AAA batteries or an AC adapter and come with an electronic remote control. The brand name "Jool Baby" is printed on the base, while "NOVA" is labeled on the seat. The manufacturing date code can be found on the warning label sewn into the back of the swing's seat.
recalls.Title: Jool Baby Recalls Nova Baby Infant Swings Due to Suffocation Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled swings for sleep and contact Jool Baby at 888-788-5665 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Thursday, and from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. ET on Friday. They can also email recall@JoolBaby.com or visit www.JoolBaby.com/recall for further details.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper design for infant sleep and non-compliance with safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a free repair kit that includes a new seat with updated warnings, new plush toys, and an updated remote control.

recalls.Description: SINGES is recalling its 3-in-1 Baby High Chairs and Booster Seats due to safety violations that pose a fall hazard. These high chairs feature a light blue cushioned seat, detachable legs, and an adjustable tray that can be converted into a booster seat. Available in blue and red, these products do not meet federal safety regulations for high chairs and booster seats, leading to potential risks for children. Issues include inadequate restraint systems, stability problems, and faulty locking mechanisms, which may result in tipping or collapsing.
recalls.Title: SINGES 3-in-1 High Chair and Booster Seats Recalled for Fall Hazard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Shenzhen Yingjieshang Trade at 206-458-7003 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email at szyjsmygswfs@outlook.com. More information can be found online at www.singes-online.com/faq-75.html or by visiting www.singes-online.com and clicking on the 'Recall' section.

Both models display 'Bubble Bear' on a sewn-in tag alongside a teddy bear graphic.

recalls.Description:
Liverpool Football Club has issued a recall for 17 styles of children's pajamas due to a failure to meet federal flammability standards, posing a burn hazard to young children. The pajamas, which include four sleepsuits and 13 pajama sets, were available in various sizes ranging from 9-12 Months to 11-12 Years. Each item features a red neck label with the size and 'L.F.C.' printed on it, alongside additional product details on a side-seam label.

The affected pajama styles include options such as the LFC Baby 2 Pack Sleep Suit and the LFC Junior Long PJ Set, among others. Parents are urged to take immediate action by removing these pajamas from children and discontinuing their use to prevent potential injuries.
recalls.Title: Recall of Liverpool Football Club Children's Pajamas Due to Burn Risk and Flammability Standards Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should visit Liverpool Football Club's recall page at https://store.liverpoolfc.com/us-product-recall or click on 'USA Product Recall' at the bottom of the Liverpool store website under the 'Customer Service' section. Consumers can also request a phone call through the recall page.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must remove the pajamas from children, cease their use, and contact Liverpool Football Club for a full refund. They should destroy the garments by cutting them in half and dispose of them according to local recycling laws. A photo of the destroyed item should be sent to Liverpool Football Club via https://store.liverpoolfc.com/us-product-recall to receive a full refund.

recalls.Description: The Spring Spirit and Biloban pack and play mattresses have been recalled due to safety concerns that pose a suffocation hazard to infants. The Spring Spirit mattress features a trifold design with a bamboo pattern, manufactured between August 2022 and July 2023. It has identifying tags that include the batch number 230315US and the manufacture date. The Biloban mattresses are white with gray stars, also made from August 2022 to July 2023, and include tags with batch number 2304US. Both products have been found to violate federal safety regulations for crib mattresses, lacking necessary warnings and labels.
recalls.Title: Recall of Spring Spirit and Biloban Pack and Play Mattresses Due to Infant Suffocation Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, please reach out via email at recall@biloban.com.

Recalled Biloban Pack and Play Mattress - underside
Biloban is printed on the mattress hangtag, and batch number 2304US and the manufacture date in the format "PD DD.MM/YYY" are printed on a tag stapled to the mattress cover

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from August 2022 through August 2023 for about $40.

Distributors.Name: Hangzhou Sharejoy Ecommerce Co. Ltd, dba Biloban, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to violations of federal safety standards for crib mattresses

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mattresses immediately, destroy them, and contact Biloban for a full refund and disposal instructions

recalls.Description:
Magik & Kover has issued a recall for their pack and play mattresses produced between August 2022 and July 2023. These mattresses, featuring a waterproof cover and marked with "CertiPUR-US" on an internal tag, may pose a suffocation risk to infants due to non-compliance with federal crib mattress safety regulations. The batch number 221002US and the manufacturing date are also found on the tag's reverse side, along with a "STANDARD 100" label. 
Consumers are urged to cease use of these mattresses immediately. Magik & Kover is working with Amazon to inform all buyers about this safety issue and facilitate the return process.

recalls.Description: Ekouaer is recalling its girl's nightgowns due to a serious fire hazard. These garments, made from 95% cotton and 5% spandex, feature a screen print on the chest and were available in short sleeves across sizes 110 to 150. The nightgowns were sold in 13 different colors, including clear blue, lilac, and pink, among others. The recall is initiated because these nightgowns do not comply with federal flammability regulations for children's sleepwear, posing a risk of burn injuries to young wearers.
recalls.Title: Ekouaer Girl's Nightgowns Recalled for Fire Hazard Violating Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled nightgowns immediately and keep them away from children. For a full refund, contact Ekouaer at 800-608-1432 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email walshkelly15@gmail.com. Further details can be found online at https://ekouaer.com/pages/us-recall-nightgown-2023 or by visiting https://ekouaer.com/ and selecting 'Product Recall Information' at the top of the page.

recalls.Description: Lovevery has issued a recall for specific units of the 2023 Slide & Seek Ball Runs, which are wooden toys featuring a plastic ball ramp included in the Babbler Play Kits designed for children aged 13 to 15 months. The recalled products have a wooden knob on the door, lack the Lovevery logo on the bottom, and do not have a green sticker on the inside wall of the door that accompanies the wooden knob.
recalls.Title: Lovevery Recalls Slide & Seek Ball Runs Due to Potential Choking Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should visit Lovevery's recall page at www.recallrtr.com/ballrun or www.lovevery.com/pages/product-recalls. For assistance, call 888-520-0167 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email ballrun@lovevery.com. More details can be found under the 'Product Recalls' section on Lovevery's website.

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 47,000 (In addition, about 4,500 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Slide & Seek Ball Run with The Babbler Play Kit packaging

Retailers.Name: Online at Lovevery.com as part of the Babbler Play Kit from June 2023 through November 2023 for about $120 for the kit.

Importers.Name: Lovevery Inc., of Boise, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to potential detachment of the wooden knob

Remedies.Name: Free replacement after registration and proper disposal of the affected door

These long-sleeved nightgowns feature a ruffled collar, cuffs, and hem, and are available in sizes ranging from 1-2 to 9-10 years in the Morning Bliss Pink print. The labels indicate the brand and material composition, highlighting a potential safety concern.
recalls.Title: Recall of Stripe & Stare Children's Nightgowns Due to Fire Hazard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Stripe and Stare via email at hellousa@stripeandstare.com or visit their website at https://us.stripeandstare.com/pages/childrens-nightgown-recall. Users can also click on "Product Recall" on the homepage for further details.

recalls.Description: This recall pertains to 5mm magnetic balls, which are small, spherical neodymium rare-earth magnets known for their strong magnetic properties. These magnets, sold in sets of 216 and available in various colors, come encased in a clear plastic case and a portable tin storage box, along with a black storage bag. The product poses a significant ingestion hazard.
recalls.Title: Recall of High-Powered Magnetic Balls Due to Serious Ingestion Risk; Available Exclusively on Walmart.com via Joybuy
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Joybuy Marketplace Express at 302-426-4543 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, by email at oversea-service@jd.com, or visit https://www.walmart.com/seller/16214.

Images.Caption: Recalled Relax 5mm Science Kit, Large Hematite Magnets Magnetic Stones Building Blocks

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Exclusively online at Walmart.com from February 2022 through April 2023 for between $14 and $15.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Yanlei Network Technology Co. Ltd., of China, through JD E Commerce America Limited (dba Joybuy), of Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to non-compliance with federal magnet regulations, posing risks of intestinal blockage, infection, and potential death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the magnetic balls immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Joybuy for a pre-paid return label to obtain a full refund.

recalls.Description: Brandline has issued a recall for HEAO's 4-in-1 high chairs due to significant safety concerns including risks of suffocation, entrapment, and laceration. These high chairs, available in various colors such as black, blue, gold, green, gray, pink, and red, feature adjustable heights and multiple reclining positions. They include a footrest, a detachable tray, a seat cushion, and four wheels that can either rotate or remain static, all equipped with locks. The brand name 'HEAO' is visible on the head support of the seat cushion and the front of the tray. A label with 'Max Lead Int'l Ltd. 3113 Glenfield Ave., Dallas, TX 75233 (302) 448-4207' is stitched on the back of the seat cushion.
recalls.Title: Brandline Recalls HEAO 4-in-1 High Chairs for Suffocation, Entrapment, and Laceration Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Brandline at 302-261-5366 from Monday to Friday, 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or via email at maxlead.us@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled HEAO High Chair with static wheels
Recalled HEAO High Chair with rotating wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of one incident that resulted in a laceration to a child's finger that required medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from November 2022 through September 2023 for about $170.

Importers.Name: Max Lead International Ltd., dba Brandline, of Dallas, Texas

Hazards.Name: Suffocation, entrapment, and laceration hazards due to design flaws and non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the high chairs immediately and contact Brandline for disposal instructions and a full refund.

recalls.Description: Two Litti City dress-up playsets, one Litti Pritti dress-up playset, and Prextex DIY slime eggs are being recalled for exceeding federal safety standards for phthalates and lead content. The Litti City premier doctor playset includes a white outfit with accessories like blue glasses and a stethoscope, while the Litti City teacher purse playset features a green purse with educational tools. The Litti Pritti handbag playset comes with a pink purse and various accessories. The Prextex slime eggs are packaged in a blue container and contain colorful slime along with mini bottles of beads and straws.
recalls.Title: Recall of Litti City and Litti Pritti Dress-Up Playsets and Prextex Slime Eggs Due to Hazardous Phthalates and Lead Levels
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Perch at 888-359-4676 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email playsets@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/playsets, https://www.perchhq.com/ and select 'Recall' at the top of the page, or go to https://www.prextex.com/ and click on 'Recall' for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Litti City Premier Doctor Playset
Recalled Litti Pritti Handbag Playset
Recalled Litti City Teacher Purse Set
Recalled Prextex DIY Take-Along Slime Eggs

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com, with the Litti Pritti handbag playset also sold at Ocean State Job Lot stores in the northeast, from July 2021 through April 2023 for between $9 and $45.

Importers.Name: Whele LLC d/b/a Perch, of Boston, Massachusetts
Amusty Inc. of Staten Island, New York
Bingo Deals of Harriman, New York

Hazards.Name: The dress-up playsets and slime eggs contain phthalate levels that exceed federal regulations. Additionally, the Litti City teacher purse playset, Litti Pritti handbag playset, and slime eggs have lead levels surpassing the federal limit. Both lead and phthalates pose health risks if ingested by young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled items immediately and contact Perch for guidance on disposal and to obtain a full refund upon proof of destruction. Perch, Amazon, and Walmart will reach out to known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: Bugaboo has issued a recall for the seats sold with Dragonfly Seat Strollers due to a potential injury risk. These strollers feature a base that can accommodate either a seat or a bassinet. The affected seat measures approximately 11.8 inches in width and 22.8 inches in length, and is available in black, gray, or forest green. Only units with specific serial numbers starting with S46012302, S46012303, S46012307, S460123010, S460123013, S460123015, and S46012320 are included in this recall. The serial number can be located on a label on the underside of the seat and chassis of the stroller. Consumers can verify if their seat is affected by visiting https://service.bugaboo.com/s/dragonfly-seat-replacement.
recalls.Title: Bugaboo Dragonfly Seat Strollers Recalled for Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bugaboo at 800-460-2922 from Monday to Thursday, 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, and Friday, 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT. Alternatively, email service.us@bugaboo.com or visit www.bugaboo.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section for further details.

Products.Name: Bugaboo Dragonfly Seat Strollers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,000 (In addition, about 200 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Dragonfly Seat Stroller

Injuries.Name: Bugaboo has received one report in the United States and seven reports in South Korea of the backrest of the seat moving downward during use when in parent-facing mode. No injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Children's specialty stores nationwide and online at www.bugaboo.com from May 2023 through June 2023 for about $900 for seat strollers only, and about $1,100 for bassinet strollers with seats.

Importers.Name: Bugaboo North America Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to seat backrest moving downward in parent-facing mode, potentially causing unrestrained infants to fall.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled seats immediately and reach out to Bugaboo for a complimentary replacement.

recalls.Description: Primark is recalling its baby rattles, specifically the "Wooden Music Shaker" and "DTR M&M Maracas" due to safety concerns. Parents should be aware of the potential danger these toys pose to young children.
recalls.Title: Mary Meyer Issues Recall for Bubba Bull Plush Toys Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Mary Meyer Corp. at 800-451-4387 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, via email at Recall@marymeyer.com, or visit www.marymeyer.com/recall/ and click on 'Safety Recall Notices' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bubba Bull Plush Toy
Location of manufacturing date code on the recalled recalled Bubba Bull Plush Toy tag

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an eye detaching from the plush toy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Independent specialty toy stores nationwide and only at www.MaryMeyer.com from December 2022 through October 2023 for about $24.

Importers.Name: Mary Meyer Corporation, of Townshend, Vermont

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to detachable eyes

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys, keep them away from children, and contact Mary Meyer for a free replacement.
